ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Lugli la, Kɔraawiizi faa ta chiŋ ni hɛri maanii.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Hɛri faa waa duula dɔɔwɛ, di chɔɔ woo. Bɔbiri tintintiŋ, faa tɔ a nii ni. Kɔraawiizi Duma faa guali u ni, di ra bar.

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Gil bɔr chaani!” Awia, bɔr chaani.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: U binni bɔchaaŋ la, di u wesso. U bargi bɔchaaŋ la lii bɔbiri la ni,

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: di ra yir bɔchaaŋ la hɔpizaa, di ra yir bɔbiri la ge hamuŋa. Bɔr duani, hɔpizi chɔɔzi, di ɛɛ a lugli hɔpizii.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ, “Gil chiŋ bargi a niiri.”

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: U ra yir kunla n du a baambaaŋ la, chiŋ. Bɔr kaa duani, hɔɔ kaa pizi. A hɔpizaa anɛɛ hinnɛ guŋ.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ, “Gil niiri la n du a chiŋ zɛɛ la chaali chɔɔ bɔkpaŋ, ka bɔ́hilii lii.” Guŋ himmaa kaa ɛɛ.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: U ra yir bɔhilii la hɛri, di kaa ra yir a niiri la n chaali bɔkpaŋ la ge muuzenaa. Kɔraawiizi kaa naa di a wesso.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Gil a hɛri lii kooree ni daazi irizi maa. Anla n na du bee ani anla n na nɔŋ di ra du bee a biine maa.” Guŋ himmaa ge kaa ɛɛ.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Hɛri lii kooree ni daazi irizi maa. Anla n na du bee ni daazi la n na nɔŋ di ra du bee. Kɔraawiizi binni, di a wesso.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Bɔr duani, hɔɔ kaa pizi. Hɔpizaa ahoro hinnɛ guŋ.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ, “Gil nine du chiŋ, di bargi hɔpizaa ni hamuŋa. Aabuu ninna bagli saŋazi ni hɔpizaa ani binni,

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: di gil a du chiŋ, di ra chaani hɛri. Guŋ kaa ɛɛ.”

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ta ninzenaa anɛɛ, zeŋhiŋ a chaani hɔpizaa, jigahiŋ ge ra chaani hamuŋa. U kaa ta chinchiŋwilaa ge jaa.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: U gillɔɔ a maa du chiŋ di ra chaani hɛri,

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: di ra bagli hɔpizaa ni bɔr biraa, di kaa bargi bɔchaaŋ ni bɔbiri la ge dɔŋa ni. Kɔraawiizi binnaa, di a wesso.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: Bɔr duani, hɔɔ kaa pizi. A hɔpizaa anaazu hinnɛ guŋ.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ, “Gil kunnyinee lii su a nii biini, zumbizi ge ni a dizi su chiŋ, di zaah ra bar.”

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: U kaa ta muuzeŋ sechɔgaa ni kunnyinee, a su a nii biini. Zumbizi ge ni a dizi maa lii di su chiŋ, di zaah ra bar hɛri maa. Kɔraawiizi binni a ge, di a wesso.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Denii u kissi a maa, di ŋɔ, “Ií ra lul di wazi, di su niiri la biine, zumbizi la ge lul su hɛri la maa.”

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Bɔr kaa duani, hɔɔ kaa pizi. A hɔpizaa anue hinnɛ guŋ.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ, “Gil kunnyinee maa ni a dizi lii su hɛri, kundɛɛzaa, kuntuuzaa ani pir sela ni a dizi maa.” Guŋ kaa ɛɛ.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: U ta pir sela ni a dizi, kundɛɛzaa ni a dizi, kuntuuzaa maa ni a dizi. Denii u binnaa, di a wesso.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Ií gil ya ma newea bá du lugɔ yaa, di du yaa duula, bá dɔɔ fɔŋŋa muuzeŋ nyinni, zumbizi, kundɛɛzaa, pir sela ani kuntuuzaa maa ni.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Kɔraawiizi kissi baa di ŋɔ, “Ií lul su a hɛri maa, di ra binni u ni. Ii daŋ dɔɔ fɔŋŋa, nyinni, zumbizi ani kunnyinee irizi maa ni.”

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: N kaa tɛɛ pir sela, zumbizi, kuntuuzaa ani kunnyinee maa paŋhoori ni kooree, á ra di.” A guŋ ge ɛɛ.

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Denii u jaa binni kuna la maa un tawɛɛ la, di a wesso werge maa, u bɔra sɔɔni. Bɔr kaa duani, hɔɔ kaa pizi. A hɔpizaa anumbɛl hinnɛ guŋ.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Too, gunnɛ Kɔraawiizi ta chiŋ ni hɛri ani kuna la maa n du a biine.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Hɔpizaa aniidaanɛɛhiŋ n wa helle, di Kɔraawiizi tuŋ huma la maa unna hum. Awia, u faa heezi.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Kɔraawiizi faa kissi a hɔpizaa aniidaanɛɛhiŋ la, di gil u ɛɛ hɔpizinaniinanii, dikuubɛɛwiai, a hɔl hinnɛ u heezi, uŋ wa ta kummaa tuŋ la. Andama ni Haawa wia

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Chiŋ ni hɛri tai wiarɛ keŋ. Kɔraawiizi n ta chiŋ ni hɛri la,

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: kooree ni daazi ni kunduulaa faa haa waa liwɛɛ, dikuu Kɔraawiizi n faa haa waa gillɛ dooŋ ba hɛri, nɛr maa ge faa waa hoo ú paruu wia.

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: Ka nii ɔɔ faa ra buluu, a hɛri maa sɔɔni.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ɛɛ hɛɛhraa di ma newee, di heezi du u miibɔɔni, u ra nyiŋ.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa ɛɛ guŋ, u kuari kambol wiifurii yembɛl banna yir Ɛdɛn biini, di kpa newee la un mawɛɛ la du de.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Kɔraawiizi gil daazi maa ni a iri lii a kambol biini, anla n wesse di ra nɔŋ nɔnnanaa, bá ra di. Miibɔɔ ni nunzuma daazi la n niŋ gil nɛr zum winanaa ni wichɔgaa ge faa chi a kambol baambaaŋ.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Muu bɛl faa pal lii Ɛdɛn, di ɔɔ gil a kambol maa sɔɔni, daazi la wer di ra hel nɛnnɛ. U faa bargɔɔrɛ naazi anaazu di lii de.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: U lugli naa banna yir Pisɔn faa ra pal Havila hɛri biini, bɔr la saliŋ n duwɛɛ.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: A hɛri saliŋ faa wessoo, tulaale nanaa ani binanaa aŋhina la yoola n kpolle faa du de.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: U naa bɛl ge banna yir Gihɔn faa ra pal Itiopia hɛri.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: U naa horohiŋ ge sɔnnɛ faa Muu Tigris. U ge faa ra pal Asiiria hɛri wiifurii paga. Anaazu hiŋ genii ba faa ra yir Muu Yufretis.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Kɔraawiizi faa kpa newee la uŋ mawɛɛ la du Ɛdɛn kambol la biini, ú ra binni de ni, ka ra paruu.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Kɔraawiizi faa ŋɔ u be, “A di daa maa nɔŋ n du a kambol naa biini,

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: ka ra dazi i waa di daa la n na tɛɛ nfɛrɛ í wer zum winanaa ni wichɔgaa maa la waa. Di i haŋ wa diu a hɔl la maa, i daŋ siu.”

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “A waa wer baal la bɛŋ n duwɛɛ. N daŋ ma hiini bɛl n dɛguu, ú nuu du.”

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Awia, Kɔraawiizi faa gil a baal chɔɔ dɔbɛrii. Unna chɔɔ dɔɔ la, u lizi u kugdaabee bɛl, ka bir tɔ de.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kpa a kugdaabee di ma haaŋ, di kpau wa tɛɛ a baal.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Ahaah! Filaŋkeŋ, n himmaa hog ni n bɔrahɔnnɛ keŋ. U sɔnnɛ ‘haaŋ,’ dikuubɛɛwiai, baal ninɛɛ ba lizuu.”

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Awia, baal daŋ gil u maa ni u mɛɛ ka u ni u haaŋ du, dí ɛɛ kpaŋ.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Haali Andama ni u haaŋ faa kpogli chakpallɛ, ka hiihzi faa waa baa lauwɛ.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: A saŋa hiŋ, hɛrikunnɛ faa dɔɔ nunzuma kaali pir sela maa Kɔraawiizi n tawɛɛ la. A hɔl bɛl, u pɛɛzi haaŋ la, “Wibiire Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ ii be lugɔ ii gilɛɛ waa di daa maa n du kambol naa biini nɔnaa?”

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: A haaŋ ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ ya ra di a kambol daazi nɔna,

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: ama, u ŋɔ ya ŋmɛnɛ maa ra dazi, di waa di daa lawaa n chigɛ a kambol baambaaŋ la nɔna gɛɛ di waa gbɔgzuu gba. Di yaa haŋ wa vɛɛ ka diu, yaa daŋ siuzi.”

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: A hɛrikuŋ ŋɔ, “Akai, ii wàá siuzi.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: Kɔraawiizi zummɔ lugɔ di ii wa di a daanɔna, ii siwee daŋ chaani, ii du lugɔ u himmaa, di zum winanaa ni wichɔgaa maa.”

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: A haaŋ faa binni daa la di na lugɔ u wessoo, u nɔŋ ge du wer siwii ni, di dɛg dii. Uŋ kaa ra nyiŋŋi nunzuma dɔɔi wia, u tɔri a nɔŋ bɛl di di, ka kpa bɛl ga tɛɛ u jaari, u ge di.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Kakpaŋ, ba lɛɛzi chaani. Ba na lugɔ ba kpogli chakpallii. Hiihzi jaa lau baa. Awia, ba kpɛlli daa bɛl banna yir fig paŋhoori, di nyɛr tɔ ba chakpalli hiŋ.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Denii bɔr n waa duani, ba nuu Kɔraawiizi gbɔga unna vol a kambol biini, ba chɔ zuu a daazi biini di suah.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Kɔraawiizi yir Andama, di pɛɛzuu, “Lee i duɛi?”

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Andama sɛɛ, di ŋɔ, “N nuu i gbɔgaa hinna vol a kambol biini, ɔnsii lawiŋ, n chɔ suah, dikuu miŋ waa kummaa laalɛɛ wia.”

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi pɛɛzuu, “Anɛɛ ŋɔ i be lugɔ i kpogli chakpallɛi? I di a daa nɔna la miŋ ŋɔ i gilɛɛ waa di labuuwaa?”

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Andama ŋɔ, “Haaŋ la hiŋ tɛŋ labuu tɛŋ u nɔŋ, n di.”

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi pɛɛzi a haaŋ, “Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i ɛɛ gunnɛi?” A haaŋ bir ŋɔ u be, “Hɛrikunnɛ dariŋ, n diu.”

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa nuu guŋ, u ŋɔ a hɛrikuŋ be, “N daŋ seei nua, á nuui di kaali sela maa, dikuu kennaa hiŋ ɛɛwɛ naa wia! I daŋ ɔɔ kpa i hia di ra tuuzi hɛri, di ra di hɛɛhraa, i de dui maa biini.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: I ni a haaŋ daŋ dɔm, ií biizi ge dɔm. U bie daŋ chanzi i nyuu, i ge dum u naalume.”

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ haaŋ la ge be, “I daŋ ɔɔ di dɔgnya, di i wa kpogli hia. Di i waa lul ge, i bɔra ra wiii. Ka i daŋ haa ra kɛlli i jaari ni. U daŋ birgi i dɔɔri, i ra sɛɛ u dɔɔra.”

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: U jaa ŋɔ Andama ge be, “Hiŋ sɛɛwɛ tɛɛ i haaŋ, di di a daanɔŋ la miŋ ŋɔ i gilɛɛ waa di la wia, N daŋ see hɛri nua dikuu i wia. I daŋ ɔɔ par di di tɔɔrɔɔ werge mɔɔ, ka haa na diwee di.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Sɔɔhri ni kooree daŋ lii hɛri, i ra di pir daazi paŋhoori ni nɔna.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: I daŋ ɔɔ kuuh, di keeni weŋ mɔɔ, ka haa na diwee di, guŋŋ i wa bir zuu hɛri, dikuubɛɛwiai, i ɛɛ hɛɛhraarɛ, hɛɛhraarɛ í kaa birgi.”

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Andama jaa dau u haaŋ hiŋ sɔŋ Haawa, dikuubɛɛwiai, waabuu niŋ ɛɛ newea maa màá.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Awia, Kɔraawiizi faa kpa sela hɔna di nyɛr laala tɛɛ baa, ba laali.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ, “Nɛra naa jaa ɛɛu lugɔ yaa himmaa, di zum winanaa ni wichɔgaa. A waa dɛg ban niŋ kaa di miibɔɔ daa naa ge nɔŋ. Di ba haŋ wa diu, ba wàá ka siu, ka ba daŋ haŋ du denii di hanna la.”

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: Awia, Kɔraawiizi faa duŋŋi baa lii a Ɛdɛn kambol hiŋ biini, ba lii la gaa par hɛri la uŋ kpawɛɛ ma baa la.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Un duŋŋi baa la, u kpa chɛrubzi, a chi a kambol wiifurii, tokobi bɛl ge n na lau niŋ, di ra nyɛgzi, di ra bar de maa lugɔ kummaa gilɛɛ wa wer na bɔr, di waa di a miibɔɔ daa la.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Denii Andama ni u haaŋ chɔɔ, u kpogli hia, di lul biibaal, di dau u sɔŋ Keen. Awia, a haaŋ ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi hiinimbuu n lul baal.”

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Uŋ kaa ba lulaa, biibaallɛ u lul, di dau u ge sɔŋ Abɛl. Abɛl ɛɛ penɔɔni, Keen ge ɛɛ patti.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Saŋa bɛl n wa helle, Keen kpa u pattikuna aŋhina, di wa tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: Abɛl ge lau u peezi bɛl biilugli di kpu, di lizi u bɔnanaa di wa tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi. Abɛl sunzaa la faa par Kɔraawiizi, u lɛɛu,

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: ka vɛɛ Keenwaa hor la. A guŋ jaa fuuh Keen baaŋ werge maa, u nyɛm.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi pɛɛzuu, “Bɛɛ n gillɛ i baaŋ na fuuh i nyɛm kennei?”

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Di i ra ɛɛ wia la n deŋlee, mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi daŋ lɛɛi. Ka di i wa vɛɛwɔ waa, a du tau, dikuubɛɛwiai, wichɔgaa daŋ lawii, di chɔgii. A dɛkkɔɔ hiŋ kpeglaa.

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
text: Keen n nuu gunla, a hɔl bɛl u ŋɔ u niwee Abɛl be, “Ya lii di bar yaa laŋgara.” Baŋ liwɛɛ la, u ga lau u niwee hiŋ kpu.

ref: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
text: Uŋ ɛɛ gunla, Kɔraawiizi pɛɛzuu, “I niwee la du lee?” U bir ŋɔ, “Maa zum. Minuu ra binnuuwaa?”

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Bɛɛ i ɛɛ kennei? A nuu! I niwee la chal chɔɔ hɛrii di wii ra duŋ.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: Awia, n seeibuu nua, dikuu hiŋ kpu i niwee, u chal zuu a hɛri wia, i ŋmɛnɛ maa wàá ka wer paruu.

ref: When you try to farm the land, it won't produce anything for you. From now on, you'll be without a home, and you'll spend the rest of your life wandering from place to place.
text: Haali di i hummɔ gunla maa, ka i kumpattaa wàá ka wer lɛɛ, í na diwee di di. Di haŋ chi filaŋkeŋ di ra la, i daŋ ɛɛ nɛkuŋ, di nyaa bol. I daŋ ɔɔ ra juuri. I volaa hiŋ wàá dɔɔ tɔna maa.”

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: I duŋŋimbuu di lii i be, di bir duŋŋiŋ lii hɛri naa miŋ duwɛɛ naa biini di jaa, di birgiŋ nɛkuŋ, maa duuribɔr dɔɔwɛ, ka ɔɔ ra juuri. Nɛr la maa n nin naŋ, u daŋ kpuŋ.”

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Ka Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be, “Ba wàá kpui, dikuubɛɛwiai, nɛr la maa n niŋ ɛɛ guŋ, n daŋ tɛɛ a hiŋ i tɔɔrɔɔ naa dɔŋ buniidaanɛɛ.” Denii Kɔraawiizi dau Keen pɛl bɛl, ú ra bagli lugɔ nɛr maa gilɛɛ waa kpuu.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Awia, Keen faa lii Kɔraawiizi be di ga du a Ɛdɛn kambol wiifurii bol bɛl banna yir Nɔd.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Denii Keen ni u haaŋ du de, u wa kpogli hia di lul biibaal, ba dau u sɔŋ Ɛnɔk. Keen ma bolzeŋ bɛl, di kpa u biibaal hiŋ sɔŋ dau a bol.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Ɛnɔk la ge biibaala faa Irad. Irad ge biibaal ɛɛ Mɛhujɛl. Mɛhujɛl la ge biibaal faa ɛɛ Mɛtusɛl. U ge biibaal faa ɛɛ Lamɛk.

ref: Lamech and Adah had two sons, Jabal and Jubal. Their son Jabal was the first to live in tents and raise sheep and goats. Jubal was the first to play harps and flutes.
text: U niwee ge sɔnnɛ faa Jubal. U genii faa ɛɛ nɛra la n zum kem lɔi ani tiitorii huuih naabaal.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Zila ge lul biibaal, ba ra yiruu Tubal Keen. U ge faa ɛɛ luuri, u niuhambiere faa Naama.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: A hɔl bɛl, Lamɛk ŋɔ u haana Ada ni Zila be, “N haana, ií ra nuu! N kpu biipɔllii bɛllɛ dikuu un duŋ pira wia.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Di nɛr daŋ kpu Keen di di tɔɔrɔɔ buniidaanɛɛ, guŋwaa, di nɛr kpu n ge di turgi n nɛkpui la kim, u ge daŋ di tɔɔrɔɔ butokkoahoro ni fidaaniidaanɛɛ.”

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Denii Andama haaŋ kaa wa kpogli hia, di lul biibaal, ba dau u sɔŋ Sɛf, dikuubɛɛwiai, u faa ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, “Kɔraawiizii kaa tɛŋ bie naa, ú chi Abɛl la u kpɛɛma n kpuwɛɛ la naazi.”

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Sɛf n wa helle, u ge lul biibaal, ba dau u sɔŋ Ɛnɔs. U de dui saŋaa nɛra jaa faari di ra yir Kɔraawiizi sɔŋ, di ra jaamuu.

ref: God created men and women to be like himself. He gave them his blessing and called them human beings. The following is a list of the descendants of Adam, the first man:
text: U faa ta baabuu, ba ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ, kissi baa, di ra yir baa ‘newea.’

ref: and died at the age of
text: Binni kalfa kabɛl ni tokko ni kpɔrgɔɔ u di, ka haa siu.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Binni kalfa kabɛl ni fidaanɛɛrɛ Sɛf di, ka siu.

ref: and died at the age of
text: mɔɔ ka u siu, u binni faa du kalfa kabɛl ni anue.

ref: and died at the age of
text: ka siu u binni kalfa kabɛl ni tokkoahoro ni anɛɛhiŋ biini.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: di ŋɔ, “Hɛri la Kɔraawiizi n seeŋ nua la, bie naabuu niŋ lizi yaa lii yaa tɔɔrɔɔ maa biini.” Awia, u sɔnnɛ Noowa.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Uŋ wa di binni kalfaaniidaanɛɛ ni tokkoahoro ni aniidaanɛɛ, u jaa siu.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: Kɔraawiizi biibaala na a biihaana, di ba wesso, ba lizi banla banna nyiŋŋi di ɛɛ ba haana.

ref: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Màá gil n Duma du newee ni di hanna la. U ɛɛ kunsiuliirɛ. Di haŋ lii zinaa di ra la, nɛr maa wàá ka di binni bɛri binni kalfa ni tokko.”

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: A saŋa ani saŋa bɛl n wa helle, Nɛfilim faa du durnyaa. A saŋaa Kɔraawiizi biibaala ni newea biihaana faa lul biizi, ba hel kpabaa. Baabuu faa ɛɛ kpeglega di dɔɔ fɔŋŋa nɛr maa zum ba wia, di lii a saŋa di wa hel zinaa.

ref: The Lord saw how bad the people on earth were and that everything they thought and planned was evil.
text: Kɔraawiizi binni di na, di nɛra wichɔgaa kannɔ ba ni werge, nfɛrchɔgaa ni biinikpola bɛnnɛ ba faa dɔɔ.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: Denii u ŋɔ, “N daŋ chɔgi a nɛra naa maa miŋ tawɛɛ naa, sela, zumbizi ani kuna la maa miŋ tawɛɛ, dikuubɛɛwiai, n biini bittɔɔ chɔgi dikuu miŋ ta a maa wia.”

ref: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,
text: Ama, Noowa waa wia faa par Kɔraawiizii.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Noowa de dui saŋa wiarɛ keŋ. Noowa bɛnnɛ faa ɛɛ nɛr la wia n deŋlee Kɔraawiizi be, u de dui saŋa. U faa ra hiŋ Kɔraawiizii.

ref: He had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text: U biibaala faa du bahoro, baabuu faa Sɛm, Ham ani Jafɛf.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Noowa faa ɛɛ gunla maa Kɔraawiizi n bagluu la.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: N daŋ gil nii wa su hɛri naa maa, kunnyinee maa siu.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ka mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi daŋ gil nuukpaŋ la n nii n dɔɔwɛ la chi nɛnnɛ. Kpogli i haaŋ, i biibaala ani ba haana maa zuu a gbera biini.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Lauri zumbizi ni a dee maa, sela iri maa ani kuntuuzaa ge gba maa iri maa, á du i be, di gilɛɛ waa siuzi.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Nyiŋŋi diwee maa iri du a gbera biini, á ɛɛ ii ni a sela maa diwee.”

ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi jaa ŋɔ Noowa be, “Í ni i diahina maa zuu a muugberzeŋ biini. N nawɔɔrɛ lugɔ ii bɛŋ wiarɛ deŋlee a hɛri naa maa biini.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Lauri senanaa la n waa doŋŋe, a bala ni a niinazi anɛɛgaa bɔra aniidaanɛɛ, di kaa lauri anla maa n kawɛɛ anɛɛgaa, á ɛɛ a bala ni a niinazi.

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: Lauri zumbizi ge maa ni a dizi anɛɛgaa, a ge ɛɛ a bala ni a niinazi bɔra aniidaanɛɛ di jaa. I daŋ ɛɛ guŋ, a maa ni a dee kaa wa wer wazi, di kaa su hɛri maa.

ref: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
text: maa ba Noowa be, di wa zuu a muugberzeŋ biini, gunla Kɔraawiizi n bagluu la.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Ba faa du a muugberzeŋ biinii yawa bir, ka a nii haa ba di wa su a hɛri maa.

ref: The water under the earth started gushing out everywhere, the sky opened like windows, and rain poured down for
text: Donii ge bizi, di ba guŋŋ hamuŋzi ni hɔpizaazi tokkoanɛɛ.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
text: A hɔl hiŋ maanii Noowa, u haaŋ ani u biibaala Sɛm, Ham, ani Jafɛf ni ba haana zuu a muugberzeŋ biini.

ref: They took along every kind of animal, tame and wild, including the birds.
text: Pir sela, kundɛɛzaa, kuntuuzaa, zumbizi ani kunzaahra maa,

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: kunnyinii maa dee, u bal ni u niina maanii faa du a muugberzeŋ biini, di jaa Noowa ni,

ref: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
text: gunla himmaa Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔwɛɛ la. Denii Kɔraawiizi faa tɔ a muugberzeŋ dominua.

ref: Finally, the mighty flood was so deep that even the highest mountain peaks were about seven meters below the surface of the water.
text: Haali u faa su, bizenaa nyubaana niiri luŋzi faa ɛɛ lugɔ nogumzi fidaanue.

ref: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
text: Kunnyinee maa ni a dizi n du hɛri biini faa siuzi. Zumbizii, kundɛɛzaa, kunzaahrijigzi la maa ani newea maanii siuzɛɛ.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: A nii su werge maa hɛri maa guŋŋ hɔpizaa kalfa ni tokkoanɛɛ ni fi.

ref: God did not forget about Noah and the animals with him in the boat. So God made a wind blow, and the water started going down.
text: Annii maa tuna, ka Kɔraawiizi faa waa Noowa ni a sela wia suuhwɛ a muugberzeŋ biini. Denii u gil peu bɛl ta kpegzi ni, a nii ra hiihzi.

ref: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
text: A muuni ni a vile ni bulli la ge n pogzee la bir tɔɔgi. Baamazi ge maa yaari, a dooŋ chaahzi.

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Baaguzi aniidaanɛɛhiŋ hɔpizaa fidaaniidaanɛɛ n wa helle, a muugberzeŋ faa jaa wa chi Ararat bizenaa nyuu.

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: A nii haa ra tuu guŋŋ a bina baaguzi fihiŋ lugli hɔpizii wa hel, bizenaa aŋhina ge jaa nyiizi.

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: di lizi dugli ha ú ga daani a bɔr na. Un liiwɛɛ, u ga du a nii nyuu di zaah ra bar guŋŋ a nii maa wa hiihzi, waa kaa sɛɛ bir ba Noowa be a muugberzeŋ biini.

ref: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.
text: Denii u kaa lizi komma, ú ge ga binni na di a nii hiihzɔɔ tuŋ gɛɛ.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: A komma n liwɛɛ, di a nii haa kannɔ, waa wette u na bɔr gba togni. Waabuu bir ba Noowa be, u tɛr u nonii di lawuu du a muugberzeŋ biini.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: Aŋ kaa wa di hɔpizaa aniidaanɛɛ, Noowa kaa lizi komma la, ú kaa ga daani na.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Uŋ laŋŋɛ annii waa be, di ga bir ba duana ni, daa bɛl banna yir ɔlivizi paŋhoolɛɛrɛ du u nua, u kpa ba. Noowa n wa nau, u jaa zum lugɔ a nii tuuwoo.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Aŋ kaa wa di yawa nua, u kaa lizi a komma ú kaa ga binni na. Un laŋŋɛ waa kaa bir ba.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Baaguzi anɛɛhiŋ hɔpizaa tokko ni aniidaanɛɛ hɔl, a hɛri jaa hil.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ Noowa be,

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: “Í ni i haaŋ ani i biibaala ni ba haana jaa lii.

ref: Let out the birds, animals, and reptiles, so they can mate and live all over the earth.”
text: Lizi kunnyinee la maa n du i be la. Lizi a sela ni a zumbizi ni kuntuuzaa ta, á wer lul di wazi su a hɛri maa.”

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Noowa ni u haaŋ ni ba biibaala ni ba haana lii a muugberzeŋ biini.

ref: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
text: A sela ni a zumbizi ni kuntuuzaa maa ni a dizi ge hiŋzi dɔŋa lii.

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Denii Noowa paagi bia hɔŋzi dɔŋa ni, di kuari mbusu lizibɔr tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi, di lauri sela la ni zumbizi la n waa doŋŋe la aŋhina kpu, di fuuh lizi mbusu di tɛu.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa nuu a sɔɔh, u baaŋ sɔɔni, u ŋɔ, “Màá ka see hɛri naa nua maa, di chɔgi kunnyinee bɛl maa, haali newea n dɔɔ wichɔgaa werge, di lii ba bichalli saŋa maa gba.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: A hɛri n niŋ du de saŋa maa naa wia, kuna duui ni a kumaa, yuu ni lumaŋa, waari ni weŋ, ani hɔpizaa ni hamuŋa, daŋ ɔɔ du de saŋa maa.”

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi faa kissi Noowa ni u biibaala, di ŋɔ ba be, “Ií lul, di wazi su hɛri maa.

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: A sela maa dee n na vol hɛri, zumbizi, kuntuuzaa ani nyinni maanii ninna ɔm ia. N gillɔɔ ii dɔɔ a maa fɔŋŋa.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: N kpaabuu di tɛɛ ia ií ra di, gunla miŋ tɛɛ ia mia, daazi nɔna ani paŋhoori, ii ra di la.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Ama, ií ra dazi di gilɛɛ waa di sel maa ni u chal, dikuubɛɛwiai, kummaa miibɔɔ du u chal ninɛɛ.

ref: I created humans to be like me, and I will punish any animal or person that takes a human life. If an animal kills someone, that animal must die. And if a person takes the life of another, that person must be put to death.
text: Wibii maa, nɛr la maa n kpu u dɔŋhiŋ, dɛkkɔɔ bá kpu u ge di turgi u chal kim. Di i kpu nɛr, i kpu kunla Kɔraawiizi n tawɛɛ u liubuu.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Filaŋkeŋ, ií lul biibaala, bá wazi di su a hɛri maa.”

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ Noowa ni u biibaala la be,

ref: I am going to make a solemn promise to you and to everyone who will live after you.
text: “N ni ia ni ii naanna ɛɛ nuukpannɛ filaŋkeŋ,

ref: This includes the birds and the animals that came out of the boat.
text: di kpa kunnyinee la maa ii nia n lii a muugberzeŋ biini, pir sela, kundɛɛzaa ani zumbizi jaa.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: A parimbuu n na ŋɔ nua lugɔ màá ka gil nii su di chɔgi hɛri ni kunnyinee maa. Màá ka ɛɛ guŋ maa.”

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi daŋ gil n kizee lii baamazi biini, di chi n ni hɛri baambaaŋ, gunna bagli lugɔ màá ka chɔgi hɛri.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Di n wa gillɔ baamazi tɔ hɛri, n kizee lii a biini,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: denii ya ɔɔ jaa liizi n ni ia ni kunnyinee maa nuukpaŋ la wia. Nii wàá ka su hɛri naa, di chɔgi kummaa miibɔɔ.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Di mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi ɔɔ wa na n kizee naa a baamazi biini, n daŋ ɔɔ liizi nuukpaŋ la n waa tunaa dɔɔwɛ maa la, di du mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi ni kunnyinee la maa dee n du hɛri baaŋ wia.”

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi jaa ŋɔ Noowa be, “Too, n kizee hiŋ ra bagli n ni kunnyinee maa n du hɛri naa nuukpaŋ wiarɛ.” Noowa biibaala wia

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Noowa biibaala Sɛm, Ham ani Jafɛf maanii faa lii a muugberzeŋ biini. Ham la mɛɛ sɔnnɛ faa Keenan.

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Noowa ni u biibaala naa benii newea maa ni ba dee jaa lii, di yaari hɛri naa maa la.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Noowa faa ɛɛ pattii, di ɛɛ nɛr la n luglɛɛ par grepu daazi koo.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Too, ka a hɔl bɛl u nyɔɔ siŋ werge, di dɛɛzi chɔɔ u tanti dia biini ni chakpal.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Denii Keenan mɛɛ Ham nau, di zum lugɔ u mɛɛ chɔɔwɔɔ ni chakpal. U lii bossee di ga kali u kpɛɛnna gunla un nawɛɛ.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Sɛm ni Jafɛf n wa nuu guŋ, ba kpa wɛjɛ yaau tɔ ba nyaŋa, di bir ba harbɔɔburumbuzi zuu a tanti dia, di kpau tɔ ba mɛɛ. Ba bir ba siweere ta bɔduma, ba gilɛɛ waa na ba mɛɛ chakpal wia.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Noowa siwee n wa ba hɛri, u nuu gunla u biibaala maa bichala Ham n ɛɛwɛ.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: U ŋɔ, “Nuuseei di Keenan! U daŋ ɛɛ u lulla yom.”

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: U kaa ŋɔ, “Ií ra chɔli Kɔraawiizi la Sɛm n na hum! Keenan daŋ ɛɛ Sɛm yom.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Kɔ́raawiizi gil Jafɛf dee wazi! Ú ni Sɛm naanna du bɔkpaŋ! Keenan daŋ ɛɛ Jafɛf yom.”

ref: and died at the age of
text: U jaa di binni kalfa kabɛl ni tokkoanɛɛ ni fi ka siu.

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Noowa biibaala Sɛm, Ham ani Jafɛf naannaa keŋ. Ba nɛra bahoro naa faa lul biibaalaa nii la sui har. Jafɛf domee wia

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Banniibuu faa soge a muuzeŋ kɛrii sugesugezi, bɛl maa ni u iri dɔɔ ba mɔɔri, di kaa dɔɔ ba hɛri ge magbɔɔ. Ham domee wia

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Noowa biibaala domeezii di lii ba liiribɔra ani ba hɛssi biine. A nii n wa hiihzɛɛ la, baabuu jaa yaari hɛri maa biini la.

ref: At first everyone spoke the same language,
text: Saŋa la durnya newea maanii faa ra mɔɔ mɔɔkpaŋ.

ref: but after some of them moved from the east and settled in Babylonia,
text: Baŋ faa izee di ra la wiifurii barii, ba ga na lɛl Babilɔn hɛri, di so de.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Denii ba ŋɔ, “Ií gil ya ma bolzeŋ, di ma a diijaŋ bɛl, ú la chiŋ di ga chug baama, ú ra bagli sɔŋ la yaa n dɔɔwɛ! Gunnɛ niŋ gil ya dɔɔ nuukpaŋ, di gilɛɛ kaa wàá yaari durnya biini maa.”

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Ka banna ma a bolzeŋ ani diijaŋ la n niŋ ga chug baama la, Kɔraawiizi tuu ba ú wa na.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Na, di ba daŋ wer dɔɔ nuukpaŋ di ɛɛ keŋ, dikuu banna mɔɔ mɔɔkpaŋ wia, i zum gunla ban niŋ wa ɛɛ kɛrɛ bɛlaa? Ba wàá ka wer ɛɛ wiimaa huhzi.

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Awia, gil ya tuu di ga gil ba ra mɔɔ mɔɔdumazi. Di guŋ wa ɛɛwɔ, ba wàá kaa ra nuu dɔŋa be, bá nuukpaŋ la chɔgi.”

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Guŋ wiarɛ ba faa ra yir a bolzeŋ Babilɔn, dikuubɛɛwiai, denii Kɔraawiizi gil ba ra mɔɔ mɔɔdumazi, di yaari hɛri maa la. Sɛm saŋa di ga hel Abram saŋa wia

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Uŋ wa luluu la, u kaa di binni kalfaanaazu ni ahoro di jaa, ka kaa lul biikpila a saŋa.

ref: Eber had more children and died at the age of
text: Uŋ wa luluu, u kaa di binni kalfaanaazu ni tokko ni kpɔrgɔ jaa, ka kaa lul biikpila.

ref: Peleg had more children and died at the age of
text: Uŋ wa luluu la, u kaa di binni kalfaanɛɛ ni kabɛl jaa, ka ge faa kaa lul biikpila.

ref: Reu had more children and died at the age of
text: Un luluu la, u kaa di binni kalfaanɛɛ ni aniidaanɛɛ jaa, ka kaa lul biikpila.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: Uŋ wa lul Nahɔ la, u kaa di binni kalfaanɛɛ jaa, ka kaa lul biikpila ge jaa.

ref: Nahor had more children and died at the age of
text: Uŋ wa luluu la, u kaa di binni kalfa ni fidikabɛl jaa, ka kaa lul biikpila a saŋa di jaa.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Denii Tɛra faa izi di lau u biibaal Abram ni u biibaal haaŋ Sarai ani u naana Lɔt lii Ur n du Babilɔn hɛri, di ra la Keenan hɛri. Ka baŋ ga hel boljiga bɛl banna yir Haran, ba so de.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: A hɔl bɛl, Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ Abram be, “Lii i hɛri ni i nɛra ani i mɛɛ dia, ka izi la hɛri bɛl min niŋ baglii.

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: N daŋ gil i naanna wazi, ń kissii, di tɛi sɔnzeŋ, di gil i birgi nɛkpila maa hiini.

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: N daŋ kissi banla maa n niŋ kissii, banla maa ge n niŋ seei nua, n ge daŋ see baa nua. Hɛri nɛra maa daŋ na hiinaa lii i be.”

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Baŋ faa ra vol a Keenan hɛri la, ba ga hel Mɔrɛ daazeŋ bɛl n du Sɛkɛm zɛɛ. A saŋa hiŋ, Keenan hinaa faa du a de hɛri hiŋ.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ba Abram be di wa ŋɔ u be, “N daŋ kpa hɛri naa tɛɛ i naanna.” Awia, u faa ma mbusu lizibɔr de, tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi, dikuu uŋ ba u be de la wia.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Abram faa kaa izi de, di la a hɛri giŋgimee bɔr n du bola la banna yir Bɛtɛl wiifurii, di ga baŋ u tanti dia de, Bɛtɛl du wiicholii, Ai ge du wiifurii. Denii u faa ma mbusu lizibɔr di jaami Kɔraawiizi.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Abram faa kaa izi di ra la Nɛgɛv barii. Abram n du Ijip wia

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: Di Ijip hina naa haŋ wa nai, ba daŋ ŋɔ, ‘U haannɛ keŋ.’ Denii bá kpuŋ ka gilii.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Baŋ wa hel de, wibii maa, Ijip hina la ra ŋɔ Sarai wessi wia.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Ijip koori nabɔnzi n wa nau, ba chɔluu di ra tɛɛ a koori, di kpogluu zuu u kooridia.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Baŋ ɛɛ gunla, denii Ijip koori ɛɛ Abram nɛnnɛ, di tɛu peeza ni nauna, kaubala ni a niinazi, yombaala, yoŋhaana ani nyugmazi.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ka Kɔraawiizi faa gil wiilkpega chol Ijip koori ni u diahina ni, dikuu Abram haaŋ Sarai wia.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Denii Ijip koori yir Abram, di pɛɛzuu, “Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛŋ kennei? Bɛɛ wiarɛ haa n be ŋɔwɛɛ lugɔ i haannɛi?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i kanni kara lugɔ i niuhambiere, n kuu guŋ wia di kpauwɛi? I haaŋ hinnɛ keŋ! Kpogli i kuŋ di ra la!”

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Denii Ijip koori faa kpa ba ni ba daama maa du u laallɔɔra nonee, ba duŋŋi baa di lii Ijip hɛri.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Awia, Abram kpogli u haaŋ, di paa u kuna maa di lii Ijip hɛri, di la Nɛgɛv. Lɔt ge izi di hiŋ baa.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Abram faa ɛɛ dɔɔnnɛ werge. U faa dɔɔ kundɛɛzaa, saliŋhuŋŋa ani salmaa werge.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: U faa izi Nɛgɛv vol di ra la bɔra guŋŋ ka ga hel Bɛtɛl, bɔr la uŋ faa felee so, a Bɛtɛl ni Ai baambaaŋ la.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: Denii Abram faa ma mbusu lizibɔr, di jaami Kɔraawiizi la.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Abram ni Lɔt n faa ra la la, Lɔt gba dɔɔ peeza, nauna ani tantizii.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Awia, Abram faa ŋɔ Lɔt be, “Yaa ɛɛ lullaa. A waa dɛkkɛ yaa n na naa gɛɛ yaa senɔɔna n na naa.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: A hɛri maa waa yaa sibɔɔ chɔɔwɛ lɛiyaa? Yá bargi. Di i daŋ la nomɛɛl, n daŋ la nodig, di i daŋ la nodig, n ge daŋ la nomɛɛl.”

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Denii Lɔt binni Jɔɔdan Waluu la, di de dɔɔ nii guŋŋ di ga lii bol la banna yir Zoa, de hɛri du wer werge alugɔ Kɔraawiizi kambol la ani Ijip hɛri. A saŋa, di Kɔraawiizi haa waa Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra chɔkkɛ.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Awia, Lɔt lizi Jɔɔdan Waluu la, di izi kaali wiifurii. Gunnɛ ba nɛra banɛɛ faa bargi dɔŋa ni.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abramwaa faa so Keenan hɛri, ka Lɔt ga so a waluu bolzenaa biini, di baŋzi u tanti diini dugli bolzeŋ bɛl banna yir Sɔdɔm.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: A bol naa hina faa ɛɛ biinikpola hinaa werge maa, di ra ɛɛ wichɔgaa ra zuu Kɔraawiizi ni. Abram n izee ga so Hɛbrɔn wia

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Lɔt n kaalɛɛ la, Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ Abram be, “Binni hɛri naa maa n barii naa di na.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: N daŋ kpa u maa di tɛɛ i ni i naanna maa, ú ɛɛ ii hor di hanna la.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: N daŋ kaa gil i naanna wazi alugɔ hɛɛhriichaa. Di nɛr maa daŋ wer kur hɛɛhriichaa, guŋwaa, ba daŋ wer kur i naanna gba!

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Awia, gaagi a hɛri maa di na, dikuubɛɛwiai, n kpaubuu di ra tɛi.”

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Awia, Abram panzi u tantizi, di izi ga so dugli Mamrɛ daazenaa n du Hɛbrɔn, di ma mbusu lizibɔr de tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: A guŋ saŋa, Sinar Koori Amrafɛl, Ɛlasa Koori Ariɔk, Ɛlam Koori Kɛdɔlayɔma ani Gɔyim Koori Tidal ɛɛ nuukpaŋ,

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: di la bá ni Sɔdɔm Koori Bɛra, Gɔmɔra Koori Birsa, Adma Koori Sinab, Zɛbɔyim Koori Sɛmɛba ani Bɛla Koori lɔ laal.

ref: King Chedorlaomer and his allies had ruled these last five kings for twelve years, but in the thirteenth year the kings rebelled and joined forces in Siddim Valley, which is now covered by the southern part of the Dead Sea.
text: dikuubɛɛwiai, binni fidaanɛɛ n bɛrɛɛ, Koori Kɛdɔlayɔmaa faa ra di kora ba ni. Ka a binni fidaahorohiŋ biini, ba faa vɛɛ u dɔɔra sɛɛi.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: A binni fidaanaazuhiŋ biini, Koori Kɛdɔlayɔma ni koora la u ni baa n ɛɛ nuukpaŋ la ga kpegli Rɛfa hina bolzeŋ Astɛrɔf Kanam, di kpegli Zuz hina ge yembɛl banna yir Ham, di kaa kpegli Ɛm hina ge Savɛ Kiriatam lɛl biini,

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: di kpegli Hɔri hina ge Sɛɛ giŋgimee hɛri guŋŋ di ga hel Ɛlparan n dugli hɛribɔduma.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Denii ba bir har di la bol la banna yir Ɛn Mispat, di kaa ga kpegli Amalɛk ani Amɔri hina la ge gba n faa soge bol la banna yir Hazazɔn Tama.

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: di lɔ Ɛlam Koori Kɛdɔlayɔma ni Gɔyim, Babilɔn ani Ɛlasa koora laal. Koora banuere faa chemme di lɔ koora banaazu laal.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Kotaal faa su a Sidim Waluu bɔɔni biinee, Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra koora faa chɔ di ra lii a laal biini, ka chogli a bɔɔni biine, ka koora bahoro lawaa faa chɔ kaali bizenaa nyuuni.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Denii a koora banaazu la foozi Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra hina kuna ani ba diwee maa, di kaali.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Ba faa kaa lau Abram niuhambie biibaal Lɔt gba, di paagi u kundɔɔlaa maa jaa di ra kaali, dikuu uŋ faa du Sɔdɔm wia.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Ka nɛra la n chɔwɛɛ lii a laal biini la bɛl chɔ di wa ŋɔ Hibru hinuu Abram be. Di Abram sogo dugli Amɔri hinuu Mamrɛ daazenaa. A Mamrɛ ni u lulla Ɛskɔl, Anɛ ani Abram faa dɔɔ nuukpannɛ.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Abram n wa nuuwɛ lugɔ ba lau Lɔtii la, u yir u dia balaa kalfaahoro ni fidiŋmantannaazi la un dɛɛzɛɛ nɛnnɛ, ba du kpeg. Ba chaali, di vɔɔ di ra duŋŋi koora banaazu la ni ba laallɔɔra luge, guŋŋ di ga huuzi baa bol la banna yir Dan.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Baŋ ga huuzi baa la, Abram bargi u nɛra la, ba ra lɔ a laallɔɔra laal hamuŋa ni. Ba lɔ guŋŋ di wa kpegli ba dɔma la, ba chɔ yaari. Ka Abram faa haa duŋŋi baa guŋŋ di ga hel bol la banna yir Hɔba n du Damaskus wiifurii nomɛɛl wiikakag.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Abram ni u laallɔɔra la faa foozi a kuna maa, di kpogli u lulli Lɔt ni u kundɔɔlaa, di kpa haana ani a nɛra baŋhina bir ba.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Abram n jaa wa kpegli Koori Kɛdɔlayɔma ni u dɔŋa hina koora la n dɔɔ nuukpaŋ la, ka bir di ra ba la, Sɔdɔm koori lii chem u ni Savɛ Waluu banna yir Koori Waluu biini.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Denii Salɛm Koori Mɛlkizɛdɛk n faa kaa ɛɛ Kɔraawiizi La N Kaali Kummaa la kizeehiŋ, kpa paanuu ni siŋ wa tɛu,

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: ka kissuu, di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi La N Kaali Kummaa la kissii, waabuu ta chiŋ ni hɛri maa la.

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: U dɔɔ jaannɛ uŋ hiinii, i wer kpegli i dɔma.” Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, Abram jaa bargi kuna la maa uŋ foozee u dɔma be la bɔra fi, di kpa a bɔkpaŋ tɛɛ Mɛlkizɛdɛk.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Denii Sɔdɔm Koori ŋɔ Abram be, “Kpa n nɛra la maa baŋ faa laurɛɛ di tɛŋ, ka kpogli ba kuna lawaa maa hiŋ foozee la.”

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ka Abram bir ŋɔ, “N teŋ hɛɛhzɔɔrɛ di ŋɔ nua tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi La N Kaali Kummaa la n ta chiŋ ni hɛri maa

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: lugɔ màá lɛɛ kunjiga maa, haali u lugɔ gessibii gba, di lii i be, í ŋmɛnɛ maa gilɛɛ wa ŋɔ lugɔ hinuu gillɛ mìŋ́ Abram birgi dɔɔŋ.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Màá lɛɛ kummaa, di anla n laallɔɔra n diwee la lɛi. Ka gil Anɛ, Ɛskɔl ani Mamrɛ hina la n duŋ la lawaa, na ba hora.”

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: Annii tuna, Kɔraawiizi faa ba Abram be lugɔ deeza biini, di wa ŋɔ, “Gilɛɛ ra ɔm. Minuu i guuri lugɔ laal pɛrɛtuzeŋ. N daŋ tɛi kinturgaa werge maa.”

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Ka Abram ŋɔ, “Oo, Kɔraawiizi La N Kaali Kummaa, ɛrɛɛ wessi ni tɔnaa i hiinaa naa maa dɔɔ tɛŋ, miŋ waa bie dɔɔwɛi? Di n wa siuwɔ, n balii Ɛliɛza n lii Damaskusii niŋ di n kapɛttɛ naa maa.

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Hiŋ wan tɛɛwɛ bie maa naa, n balaa naa bɛllɛ niŋ di n kapɛttɛ.”

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be, “Akai, i balii naa wàá birgi i kapɛttɛ diiri. N daŋ tɛi i himmaa biibaal, u di i kapɛttɛ maa.”

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi gil Abram lii hamuŋa ni, u ŋɔ u be, “Binni chiŋ di kur chinchiŋwilaa naa maa n du de. Gunnɛ í naanna wa du kpil, ba wàá dɔɔ maga la.”

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Aŋ wa ɛɛ guŋ, Abram jaa yardi Kɔraawiizi. Awia, Kɔraawiizi kuu guŋ wia di gil u wia deŋli u be.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ u be, “Minuu Kɔraawiizi la n lizii lii Ur la n du Babilɔn hɛri la, ń wa kpa hɛri naa tɛi.”

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Ka Abram bir ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la N Kaali Kummaa, ɛrɛɛ ń wer zum lugɔ i daŋ ɛɛ guŋ tɛnnɛi?”

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be, “Nyiŋŋi nalor, bulor ani pebalee, n di binni ahorgaa, komma ani erimpɔl wa tɛŋ.”

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Abram nyiŋŋi a sela naa di kpua tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi, ka jaa lawaa chaahzi dauri. Ama, u faa waa zumbizi lawaa chaahzɛɛ.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Duuni faa tuu á dia, ka Abram duŋŋaa.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Bɔr n wa duanɛɛ wiizi ra zuu, Abram chɔɔ dɔbɛrii. Bɔbiri limpiriŋ wia n dɔɔ ɔnsii wa tɔ u ni, ɔnsii werge lawuu.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Jiminizi anaazu biinii i naanna bir ba a hɛri naa, dikuubɛɛwiai, Amɔri hina wichɔgaa haa waa a maga suwee.”

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Wiizi n wa zuuwɛ bɔr jaa biri, Abram na vii bɛl n wa chigɛ, nyɔɔzi ra lii u nua, ka kacheel ra lau niŋ, di ga sela la uŋ chaahzɛɛ dau la baambaaŋ di wa kaali.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: A hɔllɛ Kɔraawiizi faa gil u ni Abram ɛɛ nuukpaŋ, u ŋɔ u be, “N kpa hɛri naa maanii guŋŋ di ga chug Ijip hɛri magbɔɔ, di kaa ga lii Muu Yufretis,

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: Hitti, Pɛriz, Rɛfa,

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: Amɔri, Keenan, Girgas ani Jɛbus hina maa n soge naa, di tɛɛ i ni i dee maa.”

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: A saŋa, di Abram haaŋ Sarai waa bie maa lullɛ tɛu. U faa dɔɔ yoŋhaaŋ bɛl n lii Ijip hɛri, ba ra yiruu Haga.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: U ŋɔ Abram be, “Kɔraawiizi ɛɛmbuu màá wer lul bie. Gil i ni n yoŋhaaŋ naa chɔɔ, aŋhinabe, i nuu daŋ wer lul biizi, bá ɛɛ n hora.” Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ, Abram nuu a zɛɛ.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Denii Sarai faa jaa gil u jaari kpa u yoŋhaaŋ hiŋ di ɛɛ u haaŋ. Guŋ faa ɛɛwɔɔ binni fi mɔɔ, ka Abram haa wa hel Keenan hɛri la.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Too, Abram ni u haaŋ Haga hiŋ faa du de, u wa kpogli hia. Ka Haga n wa zummɛ lugɔ u kpogli hiarɛ la, u faa waa kaa ra tɔgzi u dɔɔrihaaŋ Sarai la.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Denii Sarai ŋɔ u jaari be, “Hinuu baani la. N yoŋhaaŋ naa kpogli hiarɛ, waa kaa ra tɔgzi, haali miŋ tɛu ŋmɛnɛɛ lugɔ i nuu ra chɔɔ gba. Awia, Kɔraawiizi daŋ turgii a kim, dikuu hiŋ ɛɛŋ guŋ wia!”

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Ka Abram ŋɔ, “Uŋ jaa ɛɛ i yoŋhaaŋ, i daŋ wer ɛɛu gunla maa n dɛkkɛ.” Denii Sarai faa jaa kpogluu ni biinikpola. Uŋ faa ra ɛɛu gunla, Haga chɔ.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Un chɔwɛɛ la, Kɔraawiizi mɛlika wa nau bule bɛl n du Suur ŋmɛnɛɛ hɛribɔduma nua.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Mɛlika la n wa nau, u ŋɔ u be, “Sarai yom Haga, lee i liiyɛi, lee i ra lanɛi?” Haga ŋɔ, “N chɔwɔɔrɛ di ra lii n dɔɔrihaaŋ be.”

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi mɛlika la ŋɔ u be, “Bir la i dɔɔri la be di gaa sɛɛ u dɔɔra.”

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Kɔraawiizi mɛlika la kaa ŋɔ u be, “Filaŋkennaa, i kpogli hiarɛ. Di i wa ŋɔ i lul, biibaallɛ i lul. I daŋ dau u sɔŋ Ismɛl. Kɔraawiizi na i biinichɔgrɛ.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Hin niŋ luluu la, u daŋ ɛɛ lugɔ kaukpoŋlii, u ni nɛr maa wàá dɔɔ nuukpaŋ. U ni u lulla gba wàá dɔɔ nuukpaŋ.”

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔ wia tɛu gunla, Haga faa jaa ra yir Kɔraawiizi, “Hinuu Kɔraawiizi la n na naŋ.” U kaa ŋɔ, “N jaa na unla n ɔɔ ra nambuu.”

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Saŋa bɛl n wa helle, ba faa dau a bule hiŋ sɔŋ, Bɛɛ Lahai Rɔi, de haa du Kadɛs ni Bɛrɛd baambaannɛ.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: N daŋ gil n nii ɛɛ nuukpaŋ, ń tɛi nannakpila.”

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa ŋɔ guŋ, Abram chɔɔ pi hɛri. Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be,

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Ba wàá ka yirii Abram, ka ba daŋ jaa ra yirii Abraham, dikuu hin niŋ ɛɛ nɛdeekpila maa mɛɛ wia.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: N daŋ hiŋ n nua la miŋ ŋɔwɛɛ tɛɛ i ni i naanna jiminizi maa n niŋ ba, u ɛɛ nuukpaŋ la n niŋ du de di hanna la saŋa maa. N daŋ ɛɛ i ni ii naanna maa Kɔraawiizi.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: N daŋ kpa Keenan hɛri naa iin duwɛɛ di ɛɛ hɔɔhra naa maa, tɛɛ i ni i naanna maa, di hanna la, di jaa ɛɛ Kɔraawiizi la ban ninna hum.”

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ Abraham be, “A dɛkkɔɔ í ni i naanna maa ra ɛɛ guŋ jiminizi maa biine.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Di ba wa lul biibaal maa, a dɛkkɔɔ hiŋ teŋ u hɛŋ, guŋ bagli n nii nuukpaŋ dɔɔi la.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Di baal maa hɛŋ waa teŋŋe, ba daŋ duŋŋuu lii u nɛra biini, dikuubɛɛwiai, u chɔg yaa nuukpaŋ labuu.”

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi kaa ŋɔ Abraham be, “I haaŋ Sarai ge, hàá kaa ra yiruu Sarai. Ii daŋ jaa ra yiruu Saara.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: N daŋ kissuu di gil u lul biibaal, di kaa kissuu u birgi hɛrkpila hina maa, màá, haali koora lii u naanna biini.”

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ka Abraham ŋɔ Kɔraawiizi be, “Di Ismɛllɛ i vii kissi, guŋ daŋ vii du wer.”

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “N nuuwɔɔ, ka i haaŋ Saara daŋ lul biibaal. Di u wa luluu, í dau u sɔŋ Aizik. N ni Aizikii niŋ dɔɔ a keŋ nuukpaŋ naa, ú du de tɛɛ u ni u biizi ni u naana maa di hanna la.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Ama, Ismɛl wia, n nuu gunla hiŋ ŋɔwɛɛrɛ. N daŋ kissuu di gil u naanna wazi. U daŋ ɛɛ koora fidibanɛɛ mɛɛ, di gil u naanna ɛɛ hɛrzeŋ hina.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Ka Aizik naa i ni i haaŋ Saara n niŋ lul bina saŋa naa dɔŋ la, n nuubuu niŋ dɔɔ a nuukpaŋ.”

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa ŋɔ wia naa maa tuŋ, u jaa gil Abraham, ka kaali.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Kɔraawiizi faa ba Abraham benii, di u sogo dugli Mamrɛ daazenaa la u tanti dia dominua, wibaŋkanchirii bɛl, di bɔr wenzoo.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: U ŋɔ u binni, di baala bahoroo chigɛ. U izi chɔ chem ba ni, di togni hɛri jaami baa.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: Denii u ŋɔ, “Nabɔnzi, di n wia par ia, ii gilɛɛ waa bɛri ii balii ni.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Gil ba ɛɛ nii wa tɛɛ ia ií sanzi naazi, ka so daa naa zɛɛ di ra heezi.

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: Iiŋ ba ii balii be naa, ií gil n nyiŋŋi diwee tɛɛ ia ií di, di dɔɔ fɔŋŋa di kaa ra la.” Denii ba ŋɔ u ɛɛ gunla uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la.

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Awia, Abraham faa bir chɔ zuu u tanti dia biini di ga ŋɔ Saara be, “Ɛɛ paanuu sananaa kookoo tassa bunumbɛl manaŋ, di ɔɔzi paanuu.”

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Denii u chɔ zuu u nauna biini, di lau unla n pɔllɛ nɛnnɛ, di kpu tɛɛ u balii ú kuaruu, di daaluu manaŋ.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Uŋ wa daali a diwee tuŋ, Abraham kpa nail ni waagazi ni a ol yeŋ baŋ daalɛɛ di wa tɛɛ a hɔɔhra. Ba ra di, ka Abraham chi daa bɛl zɛɛ dugli baa.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Denii ba pɛɛzuu, “Ka lee i haaŋ Saara duɛi?” Abraham ŋɔ dí u du tanti dia biini.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Abraham ni u haaŋ maa faa jaa zɛŋuu, Saara ge bɛri bie lulaa.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: U faa mam u biini bɔɔiih, di pɛɛzi u hiŋ, “Miŋ zɛŋŋɛ naa, ɛrɛɛ Abraham kaa ɛɛ gil n bɔra sɔɔni, yaa chɔɔi biinei?”

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ Abraham be, “Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i haaŋ mamɛi? Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ u ŋɔ lugɔ u zɛŋŋɔɔ, wàá ka wer lul biei?

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Bɛɛ n kpolle Kɔraawiizi ɛɛu huhzɛi? N feloore ŋɔ lugɔ bina saŋa naa n daŋ bir ba. N daŋ waa ba, di i haaŋ Saara lul biibaal.”

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: Denii ɔnsii lau Saara, u hɔɔ lugɔ waa mammɛ. Ka u ŋɔ, “Haa wibii ŋɔwɛɛ, i mammɔɔ.” Abraham n suŋŋɛɛ tɛɛ Sɔdɔm hina wia

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: A baala n wa izee ba kaali, ba chaŋ Sɔdɔm barii di ra la. Abraham ge izi du baa ŋmɛnɛɛ.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u hiŋ be, “N kpa wia la minna nyiŋŋi n ɛɛ di suah Abraham ni gɛɛ?

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: Abraham biizi ni u naanna daŋ ɛɛ hɛrzenaa nɛra, di dɔɔ fɔŋŋa, hɛri nɛra maa ge na hiinaa lii u be.

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: N lizuubuu ú bagli u biizi ni u naanna, bá wer di ra hiŋ n ŋmɛnɛɛ, di ra ɛɛ wia la n deŋlee di dɔɔ wibii. Di ba ra hiŋ guŋ, n daŋ kaa ɛɛ wii la maa ge miŋ ŋɔ nua tɛu la.”

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ Abraham be, “N nuuwɔ lugɔ Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra hina ɛɛ wichɔgɛɛraa werge maa, ba wiɛɛlaa maa ge ɛɛ biinikpola wiɛɛlaa.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: Awia, n daŋ tuu ga na, di wibiire gɛɛ? Denii ń jaa zum nɛnnɛ.”

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: A baala banɛɛ faa ga Sɔdɔm barii di kaali. Ka Kɔraawiizi waa faa haa du Abraham be.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Denii Abraham hel u be di ŋɔ, “N Dɔɔri Kɔraawiizi, i daŋ ga chɔgi nɛnanaa ni wichɔgɛɛra maanii gɛɛ?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Wibii maa, n zummɔ lugɔ hàá sɛɛ kpu nɛnanaa ni wichɔgɛɛra maa kpau. Akai! Hàá wer ɛɛ guŋ. Di i ɛɛ guŋ, i daŋ tɛr nɛnanaa ni wichɔgɛɛra maa digna. Guŋ wàá wer ɛɛ. A dɛkkɔɔ durnya maa Seriyadiiri n ɛɛ wia la deŋlee.”

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Denii Abraham kaa ŋɔ, “N Dɔɔri, miŋ jaa dɔɔ bampirii di faari naa, gil n haa ŋɔ wia tɛi, haali miŋ waa kummaa ɛɛwɛ gba.

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa ŋɔ annii maa tɛɛ Abraham tuŋ, u jaa kpa u ŋmɛnɛɛ di ra kaali, ka Abraham bir kaali u dia.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Bɔr n wa duanɛɛ, mɛlikazi anɛɛ la ba Sɔdɔm, di Lɔt sogo a bolzeŋ dominuuzeŋ. Uŋ wa na baa, u izi chem ba ni di togni hɛri di willi u nyuu,

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: ka ŋɔ, “N dɔɔra, ii wa zuu n dia sanzi ii naazi ka chɔɔ, bɔ́r chaani ii feli kpa di ra la.” A mɛlikazi bir ŋɔ, “Akai, yaa daŋ chɔɔ a bol bossibaaŋ.”

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Ka Lɔt haa vɛɛ ka ra ŋɔ dí ba la u dia ga chɔɔ. Denii ba jaa wa sɛɛ, ba nuu maa kpa la u dia. Ban laŋŋɛ la, u daali diwee, di ɔɔzi paanuu la n waa yiisi dɔɔwɛ jaa, di tɛɛ baa.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Baŋ wa diwee tuŋ ka ŋɔ ba chɔɔ, denii bolzeŋ Sɔdɔm baala maa, nabɔnzi ni bichalli maa chaali wa bar Lɔt dia,

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: di ra pogzi di ra ŋɔ, “Lee baala la n ballɛ ba wa chɔɔ i dia la duɛi? Gil ba lii ya lau baa nyiŋŋi alugɔ haana.”

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Denii Lɔt lii lau u dominua tɔ,

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: ka ŋɔ, “N dammuzi, ii gilɛɛ ɛɛ keŋ wichɔgaa naa.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: N dɔɔ biihaana banɛɛrɛ baŋ waa baala zummɛ. Ií gil n lau baa wa tɛɛ ia, ií ɛɛ baa gunla maa iinna nyiŋŋi. Ka ií gilɛɛ waa ɛɛ baala naa wiimaa, dikuubɛɛwiai, ba ɛɛ n hɔɔhraa, ba sɔnchɔgii chugimbuu.”

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Ka ba ra pogzi di ra ŋɔ, “Kaali de! Anɛɛ hinnɛi? Yaa gillɔɔ i so yaa be, i jaa ra nyiŋŋi i ra bagli yaa gunla yaa n ninna ɛɛ! Yaa daŋ jaa tɔɔrii werge maa kaali gunla yaa n niŋ vii tɔɔri baala naa.” Denii ba jaa kiiri hel Lɔt dominua, di tau, ka ra nyiŋŋi bá chɔgi u gbera.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Banna ɛɛ gunla, a mɛlikazi pilli a gbera, di tɛr Lɔt du dia biini ka lawuu tɔ,

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: ka gil a nɛra maa nyuŋŋi, ba waa kaa wer bá na a dominua di zuu. Lɔt n lii Sɔdɔm wia

ref: The two angels said to Lot, “The Lord has heard many terrible things about the people of Sodom, and he has sent us here to destroy the city. Take your family and leave. Take every relative you have in the city, as well as the men your daughters are going to marry.”
text: dikuubɛɛwiai, yaa daŋ chɔgi a bol naa maa nyaamm. A bol wichɔgaa ni ba biinikpola la hel Kɔraawiizii, u hum yaa ŋɔ ya wa chɔgi u maa.”

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Awia Lɔt lii ga ŋɔ baala la n na tee u biihaana la be, “Ií lii a bolzeŋ naa manaŋ. Kɔraawiizi ra nyiŋŋuu u chɔgi u maa.” Ka u hilazi hiŋ faa fɛ lugɔ u ra duau.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Bɔr n waa chaani, a mɛlikazi faa ra ŋɔ Lɔt be kpegzi ni lugɔ ú lau u haaŋ ni u biihaana banɛɛ la bá lii manaŋ. Di guŋ lɛi, a bolzeŋ chɔgaa hiŋ daŋ huuzi ba gba.

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Lɔt faa haa ra di hɔɔzi, ka a mɛlikazi lauri u ni u haaŋ ni u biizi nonee, di tɛrgi baa lii a bolzeŋ biini, dikuubɛɛwiai, Kɔraawiizi faa suŋ baa waa ninɛɛ.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: A mɛlikazi bɛl ŋɔ ba be, “Ií ra chɔ di lɛɛ iihinaa. Ií gilɛɛ waa binni har gɛɛ di waa chi lɛl biini! Ií chɔ di ra la giŋgimee nyuuni. Di guŋ lɛi, ii daŋ siuzi!”

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Ii suŋ n ninɛɛ werge maa di lɛɛ n miibɔɔ. Ka màá wer chɔ la a giŋgimee nyuuni. Màá wer hel de mɔɔ ka a hɛhchɔg naa wa huuziŋ di kpuŋ.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Ií na, boljiga bɛllɛ dugli ɛŋ la. Ií gil n chɔ la de, di na n hiŋ.”

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: Denii a mɛlika ŋɔ, “N sɛɛ i suŋŋaa hinnɛ. Màá ka chɔgi a boljiga hiŋ waa.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Ama, a hahzi, màá wer ɛɛ wiimaa di haa de helle lɛi.” Dikuu Lɔt n yiruu boljiga wiarɛ ba dau a bol sɔŋ Zoa.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
