text: Wiizi faa futtɔɔ di ra tinni mɔɔ ka Lɔt haa hel a bol.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi faa gil niŋ ni jilbi lii chiŋ, di wa fuuh Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra maa.

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: U faa chɔgi bola naabuu ani aŋhina n faa du a kɛraa maa, a newea, a daazi ani a hɛri kummaanii faa dimme.

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Ama, Lɔt haaŋ waa faa vɛɛwɔɔ ka bir binni har, dikuu guŋ wia, u faa birgi yazi, di chi lugɔ ligii.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ chɔɔzimpikke ni, Abraham hahzi di la bɔr la u ni Kɔraawiizi n faa chigɛ di ra ŋɔ wia la.

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: Denii u binni Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra barii ani lɛl la maa, di na nyɔɔzi n na lii a hɛri maa guŋ kuull alugɔ kunzeŋ n na fuuh.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Ama, Kɔraawiizi faa liizi Abraham wiarɛ, di lɛɛ Lɔt waa lii a hɛhchɔg la uŋ faa kpawɛɛ ta a bola ni la biini. Lɔt ni u biihaana wia

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Lɔt faa lii Zoa dikuu unna ɔm de hina wia, u ni u biihaana banɛɛ la faa gaa zuu bii bɛl bɔɔ n du giŋgimee nyuuni.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: A hɔl bɛl u biihambinabɔma hiŋ ŋɔ u niwee be, “Yaa mɛɛ ra zɛŋuu, baal maa ge waa hoo bɔnnaa yemmaa yaa n niŋ yuu di lul biizi.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Awia, gil ya tɛu siŋ ú nyɔɔ dɛɛzi, yaa nuu chɔɔ di lul biizi.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Awia, a hɔl hiŋ hamuŋa ni, ba gil u nyɔɔ siŋ dɛɛzi, u biinabɔma la zuu u nuu chɔɔ. Ka u mɛɛwaa zummɛ lugɔ u chɔɔ u be ka izi gba.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ, a biinabɔma hinla ŋɔ u niwee la be, “N ni yaa mɛɛ dɛɛ chɔɔwɔɔ. Awia, gil ya kaa gil u kaa dɛɛzi zinaa hamuŋa ge gba. Di u wa dɛɛzɔɔ, í ge zuu i nuu chɔɔ. Di a guŋ ɛɛwɔ la, yaa lula daŋ choŋŋi nɛnnɛ.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Too, a hamuŋa hiŋ ba kaa gil u nyɔɔ siŋ dɛɛzi, u niwee la ge zuu, u nuu chɔɔ. U ge gba n zuuwɛ u nuu chɔɔ la, uŋ wa izee di u mɛɛ waa zummɛ.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Kenne a ɛɛ, ba mɛɛ hiŋ faa du ba maa banɛɛ hizi.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Aŋ ɛɛ gunla, u biihambinabɔma la wa ŋɔ u lul, di lul biibaal, ba dau u sɔŋ Moab. Waabuu faa wa birgi Moab hɛri hina naabaal.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: U niwee hiŋ ge n wa lullɛ, u ge ɛɛ biibaal. U dau u sɔŋ Bɛn Ami. Waabuu faa wa birgi Ammɔn hɛri hina naabaal.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: u ŋɔ lugɔ u haaŋ Saara ɛɛ u niuhambiere. Guŋ wia, de Koori Abimɛlɛk faa hum u bɔr, ba ga kpogluu wa tɛu.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ka a hɔl bɛl Kɔraawiizi ba Koori Abimɛlɛk be hamuŋa ni, u deeza biini, di wa ŋɔ u be, “I daŋ siu, dikuubɛɛwiai, haaŋ la hiŋ kpawɛɛ la ɛɛ nɛbɛl haannɛ.”

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
text: Abraham ŋɔ n benii lugɔ u niuhambiere, a haaŋ gba himmaa kaa sɛɛ dí u kpɛɛmaa, wibii maa. Gunnɛ miŋwaa nuu di kpa a haaŋ, maa kaa zum wiimaa jaa.”

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be a deeza biini, “N zummɔ lugɔ haa guŋ zummɛ, wibii maa. Awiarɛ maa gillɛ i ɛɛ wichɔgaa zuu n ni la. Maa sɛɛwɛ gil i gbɔgzi a haaŋ gba.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Awia, gil a haaŋ bir kaali u jaari be. Di i ɛɛ guŋ, u jaari hiŋ daŋ suŋŋiŋ tɛi, dikuubɛɛwiai, u ɛɛ n dɔgtɛɛrɛ. Di i ɛɛ guŋwaa, hàá kaa siu. Ka di i vɛɛwɔ ge, i ni i nɛra maanii niŋ siu.”

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ chɔɔzimpikke ni, Koori Abimɛlɛk feli yir u nabɔnzi maa, ba chaali, u kpa gunla n ɛɛwɛ bagli tɛɛ baa. Ɔnsii lau baa werge maa.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Denii Koori Abimɛlɛk yir Abraham di ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛ yaa kennei? Ɛrɛɛ n ɛɛi, i kpa a keŋ wichɔgii sag n ni n hɛri hina maa ni gunnɛi? A waa dɛkkɛ hiŋ vii ɛɛ a keŋ widɔŋ naa hiŋ ɛɛwɛ naa.

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛ yaa gunnɛi?”

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Abraham ŋɔ, “N fɛwɔɔrɛ lugɔ bɔnnaa hina waa Kɔraawiizi ra ɔm. Di maa guŋ ɛɛwɛ, ba daŋ kuu a haaŋ wia di kpuŋ, ka kpau.

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: Wibii maa genii n ŋɔ lugɔ n niuhambiere. Yaa mɛɛ ɛɛ kpannɛ ka yaa maawaa kurgi, ka n kpau di ɛɛ n haaŋ.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔ ya lii n mɛɛ bol di la hɛhjaŋa la, n ŋɔ u benii lugɔ di yaa ɔɔ wa la bɔr la maa, di u dɔɔ n bila, ú ɔɔ ŋɔ lugɔ u ɛɛ n niuhambiere.”

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Denii Koori Abimɛlɛk lau u yoma baŋhina, baala ni haana, peeza ani nauna maa di tɛɛ Abraham, di kaa bir kpa u haaŋ ge di tɛu.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Kɔraawiizi faa lau haana la maa n du Koori Abimɛlɛk dia lulaarɛ, dikuu Abraham haaŋ Saara wia.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi jaa kissi Saara, gunla uŋ ŋɔ nua la.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: U kpogli hia, di lul biibaal tɛɛ Abraham, haali uŋ zɛŋŋɛ gba. A guŋ faa ɛɛwɔɔ saŋa la himmaa Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔ nua la.

ref: Abraham named his son Isaac,
text: Denii Abraham faa dau u bie hiŋ sɔŋ, Aizik,

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: di teŋ u hɛŋ u hɔpizaa ŋmantannaazi hɔl, gunla Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔwɛɛ la.

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Denii Saara ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi tɛmbuu mamaa. Banla maa n niŋ nuu lugɔ n lul biere gba daŋ mam ani bɔsɔna.

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Anɛɛ n niŋ faa ŋɔ Abraham be lugɔ n daŋ ŋmɛnɛ wer lul bie, ú ɔzinnɛi? Ka n jaa lul biere di tɛɛ n jaari, haali uŋ zɛŋŋɛ naa maa.” Haga ni Ismɛl duŋŋaa wia

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: A bie wa helle, ba teŋuu il. A hɔl hiŋ, Abraham gil ba daali diuzenaa di di.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: u ŋɔ Abraham be, “Duŋŋi yoŋhaaŋ la ni u biibaal maa. Màá sɛɛ u biibaal naa ni n biibaal Aizik kpau di i kapɛttɛ.”

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Ka Kɔraawiizi faa ŋɔ u be, “Gilɛɛ gil i yoŋhaaŋ ni u biibaal la wia tirii. Nuu gunla Saara n ŋɔwɛɛ la, di ɛɛ guŋ, dikuubɛɛwiai, Aizik benii í ga na biizi ni naanna.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Ama, n daŋ gil i yoŋhaaŋ biibaal naanna ge wazi werge, di su hɛrzeŋ bɛl, dikuu u gba n ɛɛ i biibaal wia.”

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Denii hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ chɔɔzimpikke ni, Abraham ɛɛ ba ŋmɛnɛɛ volaa sirya, di nyiŋŋi diwee, di ɛɛ nii du hur tɛɛ u haaŋ Haga, bá kpa ra la. Haga faa kpogli u biibaal la di ra juuri bolzeŋ Bɛɛsiba Hɛribɔduma biini, waa zum bɔr la maa unna la.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Ba niinyɔɔlii la n wa tunnɛ, u gil a biibaal daapuza bɛl bɔsɔŋ,

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: ka ge vol la see tama di ga so, ka ŋɔ u biini, “Màá wer di ra binnuu ka ú siu.” Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, u faa jaa ra wii, di ra sigzi.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi nuu a bie wiraa. U mɛlika yir Haga lii chiŋ, di pɛɛzuu, “Haga, bɛɛ ra tɔɔriinɛi? Gilɛɛ ra ɔm! Kɔraawiizi nuu i biibaal wiraare bɔr la uŋ chɔɔwɛ la.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Izi la ga kpau, di fɔrguu, dikuubɛɛwiai, n daŋ tɛu naanna werge, di gil bá ɛɛ hɛrzeŋ hina.”

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Kɔraawiizi jaa pilli Haga siwee, u na kɔlɔŋ bɛl. Denii u dɔɔ nii su u hur tɛɛ a bie u nyɔɔ.

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: A guŋ saŋa hiŋ, Koori Abimɛlɛk ni u laallɔɔra nabɔma Fikɔl faa ba wa na Abraham. Abimɛlɛk ŋɔ, “Wibii maa, Kɔraawiizi ɔɔ hiiniibuu i wiɛɛlaa maa biini.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Awia, hɛɛhzi Kɔraawiizi sɔŋ biini lugɔ hàá kaa dariŋ gɛɛ n biizi gɛɛ n naanna gba. I zummɔ lugɔ n ɛɛibuu nɛnnɛ. Awia, ge hɛɛhzi lugɔ i daŋ ɛɛ n ni hɛri naa hiŋ soge u biini naa nɛra nɛnnɛ.”

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Denii Abraham ŋɔ, “N hɛɛhzi gunnɛ.”

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Ka Abraham faa nuumi di du Koori Abimɛlɛk, dikuu kɔlɔŋ bɛl u balaa n foozee u balaa be wia.

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Koori Abimɛlɛk ŋɔ, “Màá nuuwɛ di hiŋ ŋɔwɛɛ naa lɛi. Maa zum nɛr la ge n ɛɛ guŋ. Ka bɛɛ n baani haa guŋ ŋɔwɛɛ saŋa maanɛi?”

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Denii Abraham faa jaa lauri nauna ni peeza tɛɛ Koori Abimɛlɛk, ba ɛɛ ba nuukpaŋ la.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Ka Abraham kaa lizi pelorni aniidaanɛɛ lii u peeza biini chiŋŋi a bɛŋ.

ref: and Abimelech asked, “Why have you done this?”
text: Koori Abimɛlɛk pɛɛzuu, “Ka pelorni aniidaanɛɛ naa hiŋ lizɛɛ chiŋŋi a bɛŋ naa zɛɛrɛ ɛrɛɛ?”

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Abraham ŋɔ, “Minuu lizaa di kaa ra tɛi, guŋ bagli lugɔ minuu ki kɔlɔŋ naa.”

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Awia, ba ra yir de bolzeŋ Bɛɛsiba, dikuubɛɛwiai, denii ba hɛɛhzi, di ŋɔ nua tɛɛ dɔŋa.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Baŋ wa ɛɛ a nuukpaŋ tuŋ, Koori Abimɛlɛk ni u laallɔɔra nabɔma Fikɔl jaa bir kaali Filisti hɛri.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Denii Abraham faa chɔɔzi daa bɛl banna yir tamaris, bolzeŋ Bɛɛsiba, di ra jaami Kɔraawiizi la n du de saŋa maa la.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Awia, u faa so Filisti hɛri la di birgi werge maa.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Saŋa bɛl n wa helle, Kɔraawiizi ɛɛ Abraham na. Denii u yiruu, “Abraham!” Abraham sɛɛ, di ŋɔ, “N Dɔɔri, minuu keŋ!”

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ, “Kpogli i biibambikpaŋ Aizik la hiŋ nɔŋŋɛɛ werge la, di la Mɔria hɛri, ń ga baglii bizeŋ bɛl nyuu, í kpuu lizi mbusu, di fuuuh de tɛŋ.”

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Hɔɔ n wa pizɛɛ chɔɔzimpikke ni, Abraham izi bɔg u kawee, di kpogli u balaa banɛɛ ani u biibaal Aizik jaa. Uŋ wa nyiŋŋi mbusu lizaa kuŋfuuhlii daazi tuŋ, ba jaa kpa di ra la bɔr la Kɔraawiizi n bagluu la.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Baŋ wa volle hɔpizaa ahoro, Abraham na bɔr la Kɔraawiizi n bagluu la n dugli baa,

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: u ŋɔ u balaa be, “Ií ni a kawee chi bɔnnaa, ka n ni n biibaal la ende, di ga jaami Kɔraawiizi, ka bir ba.”

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Denii Abraham gil Aizik kpa a daazi chi, ka u ge himmaa jaa kpogli kizee ani niŋ. Banna la la,

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Abraham bir ŋɔ u be, “N biibaal, Kɔraawiizii niŋ tɛɛ yaa a peezi.” Ka ba haa ra vol.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Baŋ wa hel bɔr la Kɔraawiizi n bagli Abraham la, u kuari mbusu lizibɔr, di kpa a daazi sag u ni. Uŋ wa ɛɛ guŋ tuŋ, u lau Aizik di vɔɔzuu, di kpau sag a daazi ni,

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: ka jaa lalli u kizee ú kpu u biibaal hiŋ di lizi mbusu tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Ka Kɔraawiizi mɛlika yiruu lii chiŋ, di ŋɔ, “Abraham! Abraham!” Abraham sɛɛ, “Ɛɛh, n na nuuu.”

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: A mɛlika ŋɔ, “Kpa i kizee dau! Gilɛɛ kpu a bie. Filaŋkeŋwaa, n zummɔ lugɔ i ra ɔm Kɔraawiizii wibii maa, dikuu hiŋ waa vɛɛwɛ í kpa i biikpaŋkpaŋ lizi mbusu tɛu wia.”

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Denii Abraham lalli u nyuu ŋɔ u binni, di pebal bɛllɛ, u nyigzi kar daapuza ni. U jaa gil u biibaal la ka lau a pebal kpu, di lizi mbusu, di fuuuh tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Abraham faa dau a bɔr sɔŋ, ‘Kɔraawiizii Tɛɛri.’ A sɔŋ naa jaa birgi sɛɛliirɛ filaŋkeŋ, ba ɔɔ ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi bizeŋ nyuurɛ u ra tɛɛ.”

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi mɛlika kaa yir Abraham lii chiŋ,

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, ‘N hɛɛhzi n hinnɛ lugɔ hiŋ sɛɛwɛ hiŋ n nua, di lau i biikpaŋkpaŋ í kpu, di lizi mbusu, di fuuuh tɛŋ naa wia, n na ŋɔ i benii wibii maa lugɔ

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: n daŋ kissii werge maa, di tɛi naanna kpila alugɔ chinchiŋwilaa gɛɛ muu nua hɛɛhriichaa. I naanna daŋ kpegli ba dɔma di foozi ba bola.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: Hɛssi hina maa daŋ ga i naana bɛl be di na kissaa, dikuu hiŋ sɛɛwɛ hiŋ n nua wia.’ ”

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Denii, Abraham bir la u balaa la be, ba bir kaali bolzeŋ Bɛɛsiba, Abraham ga so de. Nahɔ biibaala wia

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: U hahjiga Rema biibaala genii faa, Tɛba, Gaham, Tahas ani Maaka.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: “N ɛɛ hɔɔhrii du ii be. N haaŋ siuwɔɔ, maa bɔr dɔɔwɛ ń huguu. Awia, ii jaande maa ka tɛŋ bɔr ń yau di huguu.”

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: “Nabɔma, a nuu yaa be. I ɛɛ sitɔɔrikpegre du yaa biini. Awia, binni yaa ŋmaŋfalaa la, di lizi unla maa n parii di huguu. Yaa bɛl maa wàá kpollii u ŋmaŋfalii í huguu.”

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: “Di a par ia lugɔ n hug n haaŋ ɛŋ, amɔ, ii jaande maa ka suŋŋi Zɔha biibaal Ɛfrɔn,

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: ú tɛŋ Makpɛla bii bɔɔ la n du u hɛri tuna barii la. N daŋ turgi kim la maa iin niŋ tɛŋ. Di guŋ ɛɛwɔ, n daŋ wer dɔɔ bɔr di ra hug n nɛra.”

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
text: Uŋ ŋɔ guŋ, di Ɛfrɔn himmaa sogo Hitti nabɔnzi la n chaalɛɛ so a bolzeŋ dominua la. Denii u izi chi a nabɔnzi baambaaŋ di ŋɔ Abraham be,

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: “Nabɔma, akai, a nuu n be. N tɛibuu a bii bɔɔ ani a de hɛri maa. N nɛra sibɔɔrɛ n kpa de di ra tɛi. La ga kpa i lalii hiŋ di ga hug.”

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Abraham kaa togni jaami baa,

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: ka izi chi ba baambaaŋ di ŋɔ Ɛfrɔn be, “N na suŋŋiibuu, a nuu n be. N daŋ yau a hɛri. Gil n turgi de kebee tɛi mɔɔ, ka haa huguu.”

ref: Abraham accepted Ephron's offer and paid him the
text: di ɛɛ u himmaa bɔdɔɔlii, dikuu a hɛri nabɔnzi maa sibɔɔrɛ ka u yau de wia.

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Abraham faa jaa kpa Saara ga hug a bii bɔɔ la n du Makpɛla n dugli Mamrɛ n jaa ɛɛ bolzeŋ Hɛbrɔn, di du Keenan hɛri biini la.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: A saŋa naa, di Abraham jaa zɛŋ werge maa. Kɔraawiizi faa kissuu nɛnnɛ u wiɛɛlaa maa biini.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: A hɔl bɛl Abraham ŋɔ nɛr la n na binni u diahina ni u kuna maa ni, di kaa ɛɛ u balaa maa nabɔma be,

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: “Kpa i nonii du n naapɔga baambaaŋ, ka hɛɛhzi Kɔraawiizi La N Na Di Kora Chiŋ la ni hɛri maa fɔŋŋa la lugɔ hàá gil n biibaal Aizik kpa Keenan hina naa miŋ soge ba biini naa biihaaŋ bɛl maa.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: Ka la n himmaa hɛri n nɛra be, di ga nyiŋŋi haaŋ wa tɛu.”

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Denii a balii pɛɛzuu, “Di n wa laŋŋɔ di a haaŋ waa sɛɛwɛ ú hiŋiŋ ba hɛri naa geu? Amɔ, n kpogli Aizik ú ga du i nɛra bee?”

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Abraham ŋɔ, “Akai, a dazi di gilɛɛ wa kpogluu di ra la de ŋmɛnɛ maa.

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: Kɔraawiizi La N Na Di Kora Chiŋ la fɔŋŋa la lizimbuu lii n mɛɛ dia ani n hɛri, di hɛɛhzi ŋɔ nua lugɔ u daŋ kpa hɛri naa di tɛɛ n naanna. U daŋ gil u mɛlika tɔ i see di hiinii, í wer na haaŋ wa tɛɛ n biibaal.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Di a biihambie waa wa sɛɛwɛ lugɔ u hiŋii ba, guŋwaa, i lii a hɛɛhzaa biinii. Ama, a dazi i ŋmɛnɛ maa wa kpogli n biibaal naa di ra la de.”

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Denii a balii kpa u nonii du Abraham naapɔga baambaaŋ, ka hɛɛhzi lugɔ u daŋ hiŋ Abraham nua la.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Denii u ɛɛ sirya, di lau u dɔɔri nyugmazi fi, di kpa kunnanaa irizi maa chugaa, di vol guŋŋ di ga hel Aram Naharam, di la bol la Nahɔ n faa soge la.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: A balii faa gil a nyugmazi giŋŋi di dugli kɔlɔŋ bɛl, a bol nua, di duana saŋaa, a bol haana jaa lii bá dɔɔ nii.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Denii u suŋŋi wiizi di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la n dɔɔri Abraham n na hum, hiiniŋ n volaa naa lii hɛɛhrɔɔ tɛŋ, di gil nua la hiŋ ŋɔwɛɛ tɛɛ n dɔɔri la su u maga.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Minuu chigɛ kɔlɔŋ naa kɛrii, bɔr la bol naa biihambizi n niŋ ba wa dɔɔ nii.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: N daŋ suŋŋi bɛl nii, di u wa sɛɛwɔ lugɔ u daŋ tɛŋ, di kaa ŋɔ u daŋ tɛɛ n nyugmazi ge a nyɔɔ, amɔ, waabuu niŋ ɛɛ suŋguru la hiŋ lizɛɛ ú ɛɛ Aizik haaŋ. Di guŋ wa ɛɛwɔ, n daŋ zum lugɔ i hiŋ nua la hiŋ ŋɔwɛɛ tɛɛ n dɔɔri labuu.”

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: A balii chɔ chem u ni, di ŋɔ, “Jaande maa, tɛŋ nii ń nyɔɔ.”

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: Denii u fug a nii du bol sela libee biini, ka kaa chɔ manaŋ tuu a bule ú kaa ga dɔɔ aŋhina wa jaa. U faa dɔɔ a niirɛ tɛɛa guŋŋ a nyɔɔ di wa piŋ.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Unna ɛɛ gunla, a balii faa ra binnuu tiiih di ra fɛ lugɔ aŋhinabe, Kɔraawiizi gillɔɔ u volaa lii hɛɛhrɔɔ.

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Un di kanyitti guŋŋ a nyugmazi wa nyɔɔ tuŋ, u kpa saliŋ nonibisig la yoola n kpolle du u miizi, di kpa saliŋ gbittizi anɛɛ ge du u nonee.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: Denii u pɛɛzuu, “Gaafura, anɛɛ biere hinnɛi? I mɛɛ dɔɔ dia ya chɔɔ hɔɔ ra piziaa?”

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: A toloori ŋɔ, “Milka ni Nahɔ biibaal Bɛtuɛl biere miŋ.

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Wibii maa, yaa dɔɔ sela diweere werge, di kaa dɔɔ dia yá tɛi i chɔɔ.”

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: A baal n wa nuu guŋ, u giŋŋi hɛri di ra jaami Kɔraawiizi,

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: di ra ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la n dɔɔri Abraham n na hum la dɔɔ jaannɛ werge maa. U wia dɔɔ wibiire dikuu uŋ hiŋ nua la uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ tɛɛ n dɔɔri wia. Kɔraawiizi kpoglimbuu ba n dɔɔri lulla be.”

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Denii a toloori faa chɔ la dia, di ga bagli gunla maa n ɛɛwɛ la tɛɛ ba diahina.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Laban faa na nonibisig ni gbittizii u niuhambie ni, di kaa nuu gunla ge uŋ baglɛɛ la. Awia, u la bɔr la a baal ni u nyugmazi n chigɛ la.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Uŋ ga heluu, u ŋɔ u be, “Kɔraawiizi nɔŋŋiibuu, di kissii werge maa. Awia, a ba yaa dia. Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i chi bosseei? N kossi diarɛ ń tɛi, di kaa dɔɔ bɔr ń tɛɛ i nyugmazi gba.”

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Awia, a baal la a dia, Laban pur u nyugmazi bunni maa dau ka tɛɛa diwee, di kaa tɛɛ Abraham balii hiŋ ni u nɛra nii, ba sanzi ba naazi.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Baŋ wa ɛɛ guŋ tuŋ di duaniŋ diwee dii hello. Baŋ wa ŋɔ ba di, Abraham balii la ŋɔ, “Màá di, di maa wii la wia miŋ ballɛ ŋɔwɛɛ.” Denii Laban ŋɔ, “Ŋɔ yaa be wii la wia hiŋ ballɛ.”

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Abraham baliirɛ miŋ.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Kɔraawiizi hiinuubuu werge maa, di gil u ɛɛ dɔɔŋ. U tɛubuu peeza ni nauna, saliŋ ni saliŋhuŋŋu, yoŋhaana ni baala, nyugmazi ni kauzi.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: N dɔɔri Abraham haaŋ Saara faa wa zɛŋŋɔɔ werge maa ka haa lul biibaal tɛu. U kpa u daama maa tɛu.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: N dɔɔri Abraham gillɔɔ n hɛɛhzi lugɔ n daŋ hiŋ u nua. Kenne u ŋɔ, ‘Gilɛɛ waa nyiŋŋi haaŋ di ra tɛɛ n biibaal Aizik di ra lii Keenan hɛri biihaana biini.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Ka la n mɛɛ nɛra be di ga nyiŋŋi haaŋ lii n lulla biini wa tɛu.’

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, n pɛɛzuu, ‘Ka di n wa laŋŋɔ, a haaŋ waa sɛɛwɛ ú hiŋiŋ ba geu?’

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Denii u ŋɔ, ‘Kɔraawiizi la minna hiŋ la daŋ gil u mɛlika tɔ i see, di hiinii í ga na a haaŋ n nɛra be, n lulla biini, di lii n mɛɛ dia wa tɛu.

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: Ama, di i wa la n nɛra be ba ga kpollii waa, nua la hiŋ ŋɔwɛɛ di hɛɛhzi la wàá dii.’

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: Awia, miŋ wa hel bule naa wibaŋa naa ni, n suŋŋi wiizii, di ŋɔ, ‘Kɔraawiizi la n dɔɔri Abraham n na hum, di a parii, hiiniŋ n volaa naa lii hɛɛhrɔɔ tɛŋ.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: Minuu chigɛ bule naa zɛɛ, di toloori maa wa ballɔ ú wa dɔɔ nii, n daŋ suŋŋuu nii di nyɔɔ.

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: Di u wa sɛɛwɔɔ ŋɔ u daŋ tɛŋ di kaa tɛɛ n nyugmazi naa gba, amɔ, n Dɔɔri Kɔraawiizi, gil u ɛɛ unla hiŋ lizɛɛ ŋɔ u ɛɛ Aizik haaŋ.’

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Miŋ haa ra suŋŋi a keŋ wiizi, di Ribɛka chigɔ u vii di ra ba. Uŋ wa tuuwe dɔɔ u nii wa tuŋ, n ŋɔ u be, ‘Jaande maa, tɛŋ nii ń nyɔɔ.’

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: Denii u kuli u vii manaŋ, ka ŋɔ dí n lɛɛ nyɔɔ, ka ú dɔɔ tɛɛ n nyugmazi ge gba a nyɔɔ.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Miŋ kaa wa pɛɛzuu nɛr la biihaaŋ n ɛɛu, u kaa ŋɔ, ‘N mɛɛrɛ Bɛtuɛl la n ɛɛ Nahɔ biibaal ni u haaŋ Milka biihaaŋ.’ Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, n kpa nonibisig ni gbittizi duu.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Miŋ wa ɛɛ guŋ tuŋ, n giŋŋi hɛri di jaami Kɔraawiizi la n dɔɔri Abraham n na hum la, di chɔluu werge maa, dikuu gunla uŋ tɔ n see n wa na haaŋ n dɔɔri Abraham nɛra biini tɛɛ u biibaal wia.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Awia, di ii daŋ huuhzi biini di hiini n dɔɔri Abraham, ii ŋɔ n be, di ii wàá sɛɛ ge, ii ŋɔ, n zum gunla min niŋ ɛɛ.”

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Denii Laban ni Bɛtuɛl maa kpau di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizii ba i ni bɔnnaa, wibii maa. Awia, ɛrɛɛ ya ge kaa ŋɔi?

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Ribɛkaa keŋ. Kpau di ra la, di ga gil ú ɛɛ i dɔɔri biibaal la haaŋ, gunla Kɔraawiizi n baglɛɛ la.”

ref: Abraham's servant bowed down and thanked the Lord.
text: Abraham balii la n nuu gunla baŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la, u giŋŋi jaami Kɔraawiizi werge maa.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Denii a balii lizi saliŋ ni saliŋhuŋŋa bɔmbɔga ani wɛjɛnanaa tɛɛ Ribɛka, di kaa lizi kuna la yoola n kpolle werge maa tɛɛ u maa ni u kpɛɛma la.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Baŋ wa di diwee tuŋ, a balii ni u dɔŋahina la maa chɔɔ de. Hɔɔŋ waa pizi chɔɔzimpikke ni, Abraham balii la izi, di ŋɔ, “Ií taŋ ŋmɛnɛɛ n bir la n dɔɔri be.”

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Ka Ribɛka maa ni u kpɛɛma la ŋɔ, “Gil Ribɛka du yaa be hɔpizaa fi mɔɔ ka jaa ra la.”

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Ka a balii bir ŋɔ, “Ií gilɛɛ laŋŋiŋ. Kɔraawiizi gillɔɔ wiimaa kuari. Awia, ií taŋ ŋmɛnɛɛ ń bir la n dɔɔri be.”

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Ba ŋɔ, “Guŋwaa, gil ya yir Ribɛka di pɛɛzuu zum wii la n du u bampirii.”

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Awia, ba yiruu di pɛɛzuu, “I daŋ hiŋ baal naa kaaliaa?” U sɛɛ, di ŋɔ, “Ɛɛh, n daŋ hiŋuu la.”

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Awia, ba ta ba niuhambie Ribɛka ŋmɛnɛɛ, di gil ba balii hiŋuu, ba ni Abraham balii la maa jaa lii di ra kaali.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Kenne ba kissuu ka tau ŋmɛnɛɛ, “Yaa niuhambie, Kɔ́raawiizi gil i dee wazi werge maa, bá wazi, ba wàá wer kur baa. Di gil i naanna kpegli ba dɔma bolzenaa maa.”

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Denii Ribɛka ni u balaa tinni ba nyugmazi, ba ni Abraham balii la maa jaa lii di ra kaali.

ref: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: A guŋ saŋa, di Aizik lii Bɛɛ Lahai Rɔi di ba, dikuubɛɛwiai, u faa du Nɛgɛvuu.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: ka pɛɛzi Abraham balii la, “Anɛɛ n na chem yaa ni naanɛi?” U ŋɔ, “N dɔɔrii.” Ribɛka n wa nuu guŋ, u lau u saata di tɔ u siwee.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Denii a balii jaa bagli gunla maa uŋ ɛɛwɛ di tɛɛ Aizik.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Aizik jaa lau Ribɛka zuu u maa tanti dia, u jaa birgi u haaŋ. U bɔra sɔɔni, u bampirii jaa tig u maa siu la wia.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: U nuu lul Zimran, Jɔksan, Mɛdan, Midian, Isbak ani Suua.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Jɔksan ge faa lul biibaala banɛɛ, ba ra yir baa Siiba ani Dɛdan. Dɛdan naannaa faa Assur, Lɛtus ani Lɛyum hina maa.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
text: Midian ge biibaalaa Efa, Ɛfɛ, Hanɔk, Abida ani Ɛldaa. Kɛtura naannaa faa bannii maa.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Ka uŋ faa haa du de ni miibɔɔ la, u faa tɛɛ u hahjigzi biibaalaa kuna, ka ta baa ŋmɛnɛɛ, ba lii u biibaal Aizik be kaali wiifurii paga hɛri.

ref: Abraham died at the ripe old age of
text: Denii Abraham faa zɛŋ werge ka siu, di ga chɔɔ u naabaala be.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Abraham n wa siuwɛ la, Kɔraawiizi faa hiini Aizikii werge maa ni daama, saŋa la uŋ faa soge dugli Bɛɛ Lahai Rɔi la. Ismɛl biibaala wia

ref: Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the slave woman of Sarah.
text: Abraham ni u haaŋ Haga biibaal Ismɛl la wiarɛ keŋ. A Haga faa ɛɛ Abraham haaŋ Saara yonne, ka faa ɛɛ Ijip hinuu.

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Ismɛl biibaalaa keŋ, di hiŋzi gunla baŋ lul baa, u biibambiluglii Nɛbayɔf, di kpa Kɛda, Adbiil, Mibsam,

ref: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text: Misma, Duma, Massa,

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: Hadad, Tɛma, Jɛtur, Nafis ani Kɛdɛma.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: Ismɛl biibaala faa ɛɛ dizi fidaanɛɛ kooraa, ba faa kpa ba sɔna dauri ba boljigzi ani ba chɔɔribɔra.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: U naanna faa sogo Havilaa guŋŋ di ga hel Suur n dugli Ijip magbɔɔ, di i ra la Asur. Ba ni ba lulla maa dɔm. Jekɔb ni Ɛsau wia

ref: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
text: Abraham biibaal Aizik wiarɛ keŋ,

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Aizik faa suŋŋi Kɔraawiizii werge lugɔ ú tɛɛ u haaŋ Ribɛka bie, dikuubɛɛwiai, u faa waa lul. Denii Kɔraawiizi faa nuu Aizik wiisuŋŋaa la, di gil u kpogli hia.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Biinɛɛri hiarɛ u faa kpogli. Mɔɔ ka u lul baa, ba faa haa du u hia biinii ka ra naa. Denii u pɛɛzi, “Bɛɛ wideere kennei?” Awia, u la ga pɛɛzi Kɔraawiizi, ú zum a zɛɛ.

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be, “Hɛssi anɛɛ hinaa du i hia, a hɛssi anɛɛ naa hina daŋ dɔm. Bɛl daŋ du kpeg kaali u dɔŋ, nabɔma hiŋ danna sɛɛ bichala hiŋ dɔɔra.”

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Unla n felee lii la aah, huna tɔ u bɔra maa lugɔ un tɔ wɛjɛ. Guŋ wia ba faa dau u sɔŋ Ɛsau.

ref: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
text: Uŋ wa ŋɔ u lul nɛɛhiŋ la, di u lau Ɛsau la naalume kpogli, ba faa dau u ge sɔŋ, Jekɔb. Aizik binni faa du tokkoahoro ka ba lul baa.

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: A biizi n wa helle, Ɛsau la ɛɛ kpaaŋ di nɔŋŋi pir volaa. Ka Jekɔb ge ɛɛ nɛr la n du bɔɔiih, di du dia.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Aizik faa nɔŋŋi Ɛsau dikuu uŋ ɛɛ kpaaŋ, di ɔɔ ra ba ola ni u ra hee wia. U haaŋ Ribɛka ge faa nɔŋŋi Jekɔb.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Denii a hɔl bɛl Jekɔb ra daali sigaahma. Ɛsau ge lii pir ba, di lɔzi ra kpuu werge maa.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Denii u ŋɔ u niwee Jekɔb be, “Lɔzi ra kpumbuu, awia, ɛɛ i dɔaahma naa yeŋ tɛŋ manaŋ!” Gunnɛ faa ɛɛwɛ ba faa kaa ra yiruu Ɛdɔm.

ref: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
text: Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Kpa i nabɔmuu tɛŋ, ka ń tɛi a diwee.”

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Ɛsau haa ŋɔ, “N na siwuu la! Bɛɛ tɔnaa ń nabɔmuu dɔɔ tɛŋ, di n siuwɔu?”

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Ka Jekɔb haa vɛɛ di ra ŋɔ a nabɔmuu wia di ŋɔ, “Hɛɛhzi tɛŋ lugɔ minuu dɔɔ a nabɔmuu.” Denii Ɛsau hɛɛhzi, di kpa u nabɔmuu tɛɛ Jekɔb.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Uŋ wa ɛɛ gunla, Jekɔb haa tɛu paanuu ani siga dɔzi tama, Ɛsau jaa di di nyɔɔ nii ka kpa kaali. Haali u baga waa hoo u nabɔmuu la uŋ kpawɛɛ tɛɛ u niwee hiŋ wia be.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Baŋ du de la, lɔchɔgii bɛl wa chol a hɛri gunla uŋ faa cholle Abraham saŋa la. Denii Aizik faa izi di ga so Gɛra, bol la Filisti hina Koori Abimɛlɛk n duwɛɛ la.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Kɔraawiizi ba u be di wa ŋɔ, “Gilɛɛ kaa waa la Ijip hɛri. So hɛri naa bɔr la miŋ tɛi ŋɔ i so la.

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: So bɔnnaa hiŋ duwɛɛ naa. Di i wa nuu gunla miŋ ŋɔwɛɛ naa di sɛɛ guŋ, n daŋ ɔɔ du i be di ra kissii. N daŋ kaa kpa ɛŋ hɛri naa maa di tɛɛ i naanna, gunla miŋ faa hɛɛhzɛɛ di ŋɔ nua tɛɛ i mɛɛ Abraham la.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: N daŋ gil i dee wazi werge maa alugɔ chinchiŋwilaa, di kaa tɛɛ baa a hɛri naa maa. Durnya nɛra maa daŋ na hiinaa di lii i naana bɛl be.

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Awia, Aizik faa sɛɛ di so Gɛra.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Di a de baala faa haŋ wa pɛɛzuu u haaŋ Ribɛka wia, ɔɔ ŋɔ, “N niuhambiere.” U ɔɔ faa ra ɔmuu lugɔ di ú ŋɔ u haannɛ, ba daŋ kpuu dikuu u haaŋ n faa wesse werge wia.

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Aizik n du de saŋa bɛl wa hel, Filisti hina Koori Abimɛlɛk ga tokoro a hɔl bɛl, di liŋŋi di na Aizik n kpogli u haaŋ Ribɛka di ra dua.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Denii Koori Abimɛlɛk yir Aizik di ŋɔ, “N nawɔɔrɛ lugɔ i haannɛ Ribɛka wibii maa. Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i ŋɔ lugɔ i niuhambierei?” Denii Aizik ŋɔ, “N ŋɔ gunnɛ dikuu minna ɔm, nɛbɛl gilɛɛ wa kpuŋ ka kpau wia.”

ref: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
text: Koori Abimɛlɛk n wa nuu guŋ, u ŋɔ, “Bɛɛ wiarɛ i ŋɔ guŋ tɛɛ yaai? I daŋ faa gil nɛbɛl wa ɛɛ wichɔgaa werge maa, di lau i haaŋ chɔɔ. I daŋ faa gil ya di bilchɔg.”

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: Denii Koori Abimɛlɛk faa karfi u nɛra di ŋɔ, “Nɛr la maa n niŋ ɛɛ Aizik ni u haaŋ naa wichɔgii maa, dɛkkɔɔ uŋ siuwɛ!”

ref: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
text: Aizik faa duu kuna a hɛri, a bina hiŋ biini, u pattikuna faa lɛɛ werge naa bukalfa, u kumaa, dikuu Kɔraawiizi n hiinuu wia.

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Denii u faa birgi dɔɔŋ kakpaŋ, u kundɔɔlaa ra wazi, u wa birgi dɔɔŋ werge.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: Uŋ faa dɔɔ balaa ni sela werge maa la wia, a gunna wii Filisti hina ni.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: Ba kuu guŋ wia di gbaari hɛɛhraa tɔɔgi Aizik kɔlɔnzi la maa u mɛɛ Abraham balaa n faa kiwee u de dui saŋa la.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Koori Abimɛlɛk ŋɔ Aizik be, “Izi yaa hɛri. I jaa dɔɔ fɔŋŋaa werge di kaali yaa.”

ref: Isaac left and settled in Gerar Valley,
text: Awia, u faa izi de di la Gɛra Waluu di ga so.

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Denii u kaa pilli a kɔlɔnzi la maa Filisti hina n faa tɔɔgɛɛ saŋa la u mɛɛ Abraham n wa siuwɛ la. Aizik faa kaa ra yir a maa ni a sɔna gunla u mɛɛ n faa dawaa la.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: U balaa ge faa kaa ki kɔlɔŋ bɛl a waluu biini di na niinanii.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: Saŋa bɛl n wa helle, Gɛra senɔɔna ni Aizik senɔɔna ra naa a nii wia, di ŋɔ, “Nii naa ɛɛ yaa hotte.” Awia, Aizik faa dau a kɔlɔŋ sɔŋ Ɛsɛk.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Aizik balaa la faa kaa ki a kɔlɔŋ bɛl. Ka baŋ wa kiu la, ba ni a nɛra faa kaa ra naa u ge wia. Aizik faa kaa dau u ge sɔŋ Sitna.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Ba kaa lii de di ga ki bɛl. Baŋ wa ki waabuu, ba ni Filisti hina waa kaa naa dikuu u wia. Denii Aizik faa jaa dau waabuu sɔŋ Rɛhɔbɔf di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi jaa hiini yaabuu, yaa wer dɔɔ yaa hinaa. Yaa daŋ jaa wer so hɛri naa di nyɛɛli.”

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Denii Aizik kaa lii de di ga so bolzeŋ Bɛɛsiba.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: A hamuŋa ni, Kɔraawiizi ba u be di wa ŋɔ, “Minuu Kɔraawiizi la i mɛɛ Abraham n na hum la. Awia, gilɛɛ ra ɔm, dikuubɛɛwiai, n du i benii saŋa maa n na hiinii. N daŋ gil i naanna wazi werge maa, dikuu nua la miŋ faa ŋɔwɛɛ tɛɛ n balii Abraham la wia.”

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Aizik faa ma mbusu lizibɔr de, di ra jaami Kɔraawiizi. U kaa baŋzi u tanti diini, di gil u balaa ki kɔlɔŋ.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
text: A hɔl bɛl, Koori Abimɛlɛk ni u zɛɛ sogna nabɔma Ahuzaf ani u laallɔɔra nabɔma Fikɔl lii Gɛra di ba Aizik be.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Denii Aizik pɛɛzi baa, “Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ ii ba n benei? Ii zummɔ lugɔ ii faa hahmbuu di duŋŋiŋ lii ii hɛri.”

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Denii ba ŋɔ, “Yaa nawɔɔrɛ wibii maa lugɔ Kɔraawiizi du i benii. Awiarɛ yaa ra nyiŋŋi yaa nii hɛɛhzi di ɛɛ nuukpaŋ

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: lugɔ hàá ɛɛ yaa wichɔgii maa gunla yaa n wɛɛ ɛɛwɛ wichɔgii maa la. Hiŋ faa du ya be la, yaa faa ra ɛɛibuu nɛnnɛ. Haali yaa n kaa waa lizii yaa be la gba, yaa gillɔɔ i kaali ani bampirsɔna. Awia, filaŋkeŋ, yaa nawɔɔ lugɔ Kɔraawiizi jaa hiiniibuu werge maa.”

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Aizik gil ba daali diuzenaa tɛɛ baa ba di, di nyɔɔ.

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: Hɔɔŋ waa pizi chɔɔzimpikke ni, bɛl maa hɛɛhzi lugɔ wàá ka ɛɛ u dɔŋ wichɔgii. Aizik jaa ta baa ŋmɛnɛɛ, ba bir kaali ni bampirsɔna.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: A hɔl himmaanii u balaa ba di wa ŋɔ, “Yaa ki kɔlɔnnɛ, di na nii de.”

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Denii Aizik faa dau a kɔlɔŋ sɔŋ Siba. Haali ni zinaa a bolzeŋ sɔnnɛ, Bɛɛsiba.

ref: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Ba wia faa gillɔɔ Aizik ni u haaŋ Ribɛka biine faa chɔgi werge maa.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Aizik n wa zɛŋŋɛ werge, waa kaa ra na nɛnnɛ, u yir u biinabɔma Ɛsau, di ŋɔ, “N biibaal,” Ɛsau sɛɛ, di ŋɔ, “Minuu keŋ.”

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Aizik ŋɔ, “Filaŋkennaa, n zɛŋŋɔɔ werge, maa zum hɔpizii la min niŋ siu.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Awia, kpa i hoo di zuu pir di ga nyiŋŋi dɔzi,

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: di daaluu gunla minna nyiŋŋi la, di wa tɛŋ ń hee, ka kissii mɔɔ, ka siu.”

ref: Rebekah had been listening, and as soon as Esau left to go hunting,
text: Ka banna ŋɔ wia la, di Ribɛka pɛlɔɔ di ra nuu a maa. Ɛsau n wa liwɛɛ kaali pir la,

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Ribɛka ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “N nuuwɔɔ i mɛɛ n ŋɔ Ɛsau be

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: lugɔ ú ga nyiŋŋi pir ol di wa daali diunanaa tɛu, ú di, ka kissuu Kɔraawiizi sibɔɔ mɔɔ, ka siu.

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: Awia, n biibaal, keŋŋi digna di ra nuu nɛnnɛ.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: La ga binni sela la biini di lau bupɔlnanaa anɛɛ ba, ya kpaa daali diunanaa la i mɛɛ n na nyiŋŋi werge la.

ref: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
text: Di n wa daalɔɔ tuŋ, í kpaa la ga tɛɛ i mɛɛ ú di, ka kissii Kɔraawiizi sibɔɔ mɔɔ, ka siu.”

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Denii Jekɔb bir ŋɔ u maa be, “N ma, i zum gunla Ɛsau n dɔɔ huna, ka maa huna dɔɔwɛ.

ref: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
text: Di n mɛɛ wa largiŋ na, u daŋ zum lugɔ n na daruubuu, di gil n kissaa ka seeŋ nua.”

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Ka Ribɛka ŋɔ, “Di a nuuseei waa ba, minuu a na. Awia, hiŋwaa ɛɛ gunla miŋ baglii naa. Lii ga nyiŋŋi a buuna ba.”

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Jekɔb faa jaa nuu u maa wiŋɔzi la, di lii ga lau a buuna anɛɛ ba, Ribɛka kpaa di daali diunanaa, gunla Aizik n ɔɔ ra nyiŋŋi la.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Denii u kpa Ɛsau laala la n wesse werge di tɛɛ Jekɔb u laali.

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: U faa kpa a buuna hɔna di tɔ u vaama ni u baŋaa,

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: ka jaa kpa a diunanaa ni paanuu uŋ ɔɔzɛɛ tɛɛ Jekɔb.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Jekɔb kpa a diwee di la u mɛɛ be, di ga ŋɔ, “N mɛ!” Aizik ŋɔ, “N biibaal.” Ka bir pɛɛzi, “Anɛɛwɛi?”

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Jekɔb na manaŋ ŋɔ, “I biinabɔma Ɛsaurɛ. N ɛɛ gunla maa hiŋ bagliŋ labuu. Izi so di hee a ol yeŋ ka kissiŋ.”

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Aizik pɛɛzuu, “Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛ di nau manaŋ kennei?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la hinna hum labuu hiiniŋ.”

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Denii Aizik ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “Ba n be bɔnnaa. N na nyiŋŋuu ń largi i bɔrahɔŋ na, di Ɛsaurɛ hiŋ wibii maa gɛɛ?”

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Jekɔb la u be, u ga larguu di na, ka ŋɔ, “I loobii ra lii Jekɔb loobiire, ka i vaama waa ɛɛ Ɛsau vaama.”

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: “Hinuu Ɛsau wibii maa?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Ɛɛh, minuu wibii maa.”

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Denii Aizik jaa ŋɔ, “Kpa a ol tɛŋ ń hee ka kissii.” Jekɔb kpa a ol tɛɛ u mɛɛ, u hee, di kaa kpa siŋ di tɛu, u nyɔɔ.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: Aizik kaa ŋɔ, “Ba ɛŋ di wa faamiŋ, n biibaal.”

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Denii Jekɔb la ga faamuu. Uŋ ga faamuu la, u nuu u laala la sɔɔh, ka faa jaa kissuu di ŋɔ, “N biibaal sɔɔh summɔɔ alugɔ pir yembɛl Kɔraawiizi n kisseere.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Kɔ́raawiizi ɔɔ tɛi sɔnii di gil i pattikuna lɛɛ, í na mia ni siŋ werge.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Kɔ́raawiizi gil hɛrkpila maa nɛra ɛɛ i balaa di ra tognii. I daŋ ɛɛ i lulla maa dɔɔri bá ge ra tognii. Di banla maa wa seei nua, Kɔraawiizi daŋ see baa nua. Di banla maa ge kaa wa kissii, Kɔraawiizi daŋ kissi baa.”

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Aizik n haŋ wa kissuu ka u lii kaali, a waa birgee ka u kpɛɛma Ɛsau ge lii pir ba.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Denii u ge daali diwee la u mɛɛ n na nyiŋŋi werge la di wa tɛu, ka ŋɔ u be, “N mɛ, izi so di hee a ol yeŋ, ka kissiŋ.”

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Aizik pɛɛzuu, “Anɛɛ hinnɛi?” Ɛsau ŋɔ, “Minuu i biinabɔma Ɛsau.”

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: Aizik n wa nuu guŋ, u bɔra ra sa werge, u ŋɔ, “Ka anɛɛ n kpu ol di daali diwee di wa tɛŋ lai? N diabuu tama ka i haŋ ba la. N teŋ kissuubuu, a waa kaa dɔɔ biraa maa.”

ref: Esau cried out in great distress, “Father, give me a blessing too!”
text: Ɛsau n wa nuu guŋ, u ra chɛɛhgi di ra ŋɔ, “N mɛ, kissi n ge!”

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Ka Aizik ŋɔ, “I niwee ballɔɔ di wa dariŋ, n kpa a kissaa la maa tɛu.”

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Denii Ɛsau ŋɔ, “Guŋ wiarɛ u sɔŋ di Jekɔb la, dikuubɛɛwiai, u darimbuu bunɛɛ. U feloore lɛɛ n nabɔmuu, di kaa lɛɛ n kissaa naa ge jaa.” Denii u kaa pɛɛzi u mɛɛ, “Amɔ, kissaa maa waa kaa hoo hin niŋ kissi n gea?”

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Aizik ŋɔ Ɛsau be, “N teŋ gillɔɔ Jekɔb birgi i dɔɔri, di kaa ŋɔ lugɔ u kpɛɛnna ni u niuna maa daŋ ɛɛ u balaa di ra humuu. N tɛubuu mia ni siŋ. Awia, kissaa maa waa kaa hoo min niŋ kissii, n biibaal!”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ɛsau haa vɛɛ suŋŋuu, di ŋɔ, “Amɔ, kissikpaŋ bɛnnɛ i dɔɔ di ra kissi nɛraa? N mɛ, kissi n ge gba!” Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, u ra chɛɛhgi.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Denii u mɛɛ Aizik ŋɔ u be, “I duuribɔr wàá dɔɔ sɔnii, i hɛri ge waa du sum.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: I daŋ birgi laallɔɔri, di hum ra tɛɛ i niwee. Ama, di i bɔra wa nyɛkkɔ u dɔɔra sɛɛi wia, i daŋ bargi i hiŋ di lii u be.” Jekɔb n chɔwɛɛ kaali Laban be wia

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Ɛsau faa jaa hah Jekɔb werge maa, dikuu un dar u mɛɛ u kissuu la wia. U ŋɔ, “N mɛɛ daŋ siu filaŋkeŋ maa, ka ń na bɔr di kpu Jekɔb.”

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Baŋ wa ŋɔ Ribɛka be gunla u biinabɔma Ɛsau n ŋɔwɛɛ la, u yir Jekɔb, di ŋɔ u be, “Ɛsau ra nyiŋŋi i kpuirɛ.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Awia, n biibaal, a nuu n be. Izi manaŋ di chɔ di ra la n niubambie Laban be, Haran hɛri.

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Gilɛɛ waa ba di u baaŋ waa sɔɔnɛɛ.

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: Di u wa sɔɔnɔɔ, u suuh gunla hiŋ ɛɛwɛ la, n daŋ yirii. Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ n nyaa ii maa hɔpizikpanɛi?”

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Ribɛka ŋɔ Aizik be, “Hitti hina biihaana naa Ɛsau n kpawɛɛ naa wia ra lii n lɔgɔɔ. Awia, miŋ siuwɛ bɔ miŋ du de di na Jekɔb ge n kpa ba bɛl.”

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Denii Aizik yir Jekɔb di kissuu, ka ŋɔ u be, “Gilɛɛ waa kpa Keenan hina biihaaŋ maa.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: La Mɛsɔpɔtemia, i naabaal Bɛtuɛl nɛra be, di ga kpa i naara Laban biihaana la bɛl.

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Kɔ́raawiizi Fɔŋŋa Maa Hiŋ la hiinii, di tɛi biikpila, di gil i birgi hɛrkpila hina mɛɛ.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Kɔ́raawiizi kissi i ni i naanna gunla uŋ kissi Abraham la, di gil ii dɔɔ hɛri naa yaa n duwɛɛ di ɛɛ hɔɔhra, Kɔraawiizi n felee kpau tɛɛ Abraham naa fɔŋŋa.”

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Denii Aizik faa gil Jekɔb kaali Mɛsɔpɔtemia di ga du u naara Laban be. A Laban hiŋ faa ɛɛ Bɛtuɛl n ɛɛ Siiria hinuu la biibaallɛ, di ka ɛɛ Ribɛka ge niubambie. Ribɛka ge ɛɛ Jekɔb ni Ɛsau maa.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Ɛsau nuu lugɔ u mɛɛ kissi Jekɔbuu, di gil u kaali u naara be Mɛsɔpɔtemia ú ga nyiŋŋi haaŋ, di kaa nuu lugɔ u karfi Jekɔbuu lugɔ u gilɛɛ waa kpa Keenan hina biihaaŋ maa,

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: di kaa zum lugɔ Jekɔb nuu u maa ni u mɛɛ wiŋɔzii, di kaali Mɛsɔpɔtemia.

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: Denii Ɛsau jaa zum lugɔ wibii maa, u mɛɛ waa Keenan hina biihaana nɔŋŋɛɛ.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: Awia, u faa la u naara Ismɛl be di ga kpa u biihaaŋ Mahalaf, n ɛɛ Nɛbayɔf niuhambie, di kaa ɛɛ Abraham biibaal Ismɛl ge biihaaŋ, di wa jaa u haana ni. Jekɔb n deezee Bɛtɛl

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Jekɔb faa lii Bɛɛsiba di ra la Haran.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Wiizi n waa chol, di u hel yembɛl, u chɔɔ de a hamuŋa hiŋ. Denii u kpa bii di birgi u kaputti di sil, di ra chɔɔ dɔɔ.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Unna chɔɔ dɔɔ la, u deezi di na keeraa bɛl n chigɛ hɛri di ga chug chiŋ, mɛlikazi ga u ni di ra tinni, di kaa ra tuu.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Kɔraawiizi chi u kɛrii di ra ŋɔ, “Minuu Kɔraawiizi la i naabaala Abraham ni Aizik n faa ra hum la. Hɛri naa hiŋ chɔɔ u ni naa ɛɛ i hotte. N daŋ kpau tɛɛ i ni i naanna.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: I naanna daŋ wazi werge maa di su hɛri naa maa gunla nyɔɔ n ɔɔ tɔ bɔr maa la. Ba daŋ su di lii wiifurii di wa kaali wiicholii, di kaa lii wiifurii nomɛɛl wiikakag di wa kaali wiifurii nodig wiikakag. Hɛri naa nɛra maa daŋ na hiinaa werge, di lii i naanna be.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: N daŋ ɔɔ du i be saŋa maa di ra hiinii, bɔr la maa hinna la. Ka ŋmɛnɛ bɛl n daŋ kaa gil i bir ba hɛri naa. N daŋ ɔɔ du i be saŋaa saŋa maa, di ra ba saŋa la min niŋ wa tɛi kunla maa miŋ ŋɔ nua u wia la.”

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Denii Jekɔb hassi izi di ŋɔ, “Wibii maa, Kɔraawiizi du ɛnnɛ, ka n faa waa zummɛ.”

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Denii ɔnsii lawuu, u ŋɔ, “Bɔnnaa wia dɔɔ ɔnsiirɛ werge. Kɔraawiizi duuribɔttɛ, wibii maa. U ɛɛ Kɔraawiizi bol dominuuzenne.”

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Hɔɔŋ waa pizi chɔɔzimpikke ni, u feli izi di kpa bii la uŋ kpawɛɛ sil la di baŋ, ú ɛɛ daansɛɛ kuŋ. Uŋ wa baŋuu tuŋ, u yiili nuu di waari u ni,

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: ka jaa dau de sɔŋ Bɛtɛl, haali baŋ faa ra yir a bolzeŋ Luuz la.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Denii Jekɔb lɛɛ nua Kɔraawiizi be, di ŋɔ, “Di Kɔraawiizi daŋ hiiniŋ n ŋmɛnɛɛ naa biini, di tɛŋ diwee ni laala,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: di kaa biriŋ di ba n mɛɛ be ani bɔsɔna, guŋwaa, u daŋ ɛɛ Kɔraawiizi la min ninna hum.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Bii naa miŋ baŋŋɛ naa ge daŋ ɛɛ bɔr la ban ninna jaamuu. Denii ń jaa kpa kuna la miŋ dɔɔwɛ fi bɔkpaŋ di tɛu.”

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Denii Jekɔb kaa kpa ŋmɛnɛɛ di ra kaali, di ga hel wiifurii hina hɛri,

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: di na kɔlɔŋ bɛl, di peezaa chɔɔ sugezi bɔra ahoro, di dugluu, dikuubɛɛwiai, a kɔlɔnnɛ ba ɔɔ dɔɔ nii di ra tɛɛa. Bii la ge n tɔ a kɔlɔŋ nua la faa kannɔɔ.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Di a peeza maa wa chaalɔɔ, penɔɔna ɔɔ billi a biirɛ, ka ɛɛ nii tɛɛa, a nyɔɔ, ka ba bir billuu tɔ a kɔlɔŋ nua.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Jekɔb la a penɔɔna be di ga pɛɛzi baa, “N dammuzi, lee ii liiyɛi?” Ba ŋɔ, “Harannɛ yaa lii.”

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: U kaa pɛɛzi baa, “Ií zum Nahɔ naana la banna yir Laban laa?” Ba ŋɔ “Ɛɛh, yaa zumuubuu.”

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Jekɔb kaa pɛɛzi, “U du alaanfiaa?” Ba ŋɔ, “Ɛɛh, u du alaanfia. Na, u biihaaŋ Rachɛllɛ nɔɔni peeza di ra ba la.”

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Wiizi haa kannɔɔ, peeza tɔi haa waa helle. Ií ɛɛ nii tɛɛa, ka gil á la gaa di.”

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Denii ba ŋɔ, “See a peeza maa n chaalɛɛ, bá billi a bii lii a kɔlɔŋ nua, ka ya tɛɛa nii.”

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Jekɔb ni baa n na ŋɔ wia la, di Rachɛl nɔɔni u mɛɛ peeza di wa hel. U faa ɛɛ biihaannɛ ka ra nɔɔni a peeza.

ref: When Jacob saw her and his uncle's sheep, he rolled the rock away and watered the sheep.
text: Rachɛl n faa ɛɛ u naara biihaaŋ la, a peeza ge ɛɛ u naara hora la wia, u la ga lau a bii billi ha, ka ɛɛ a nii tɛɛ a peeza a nyɔɔ.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Uŋ wa tɛɛa tuŋ, u la ga faami Rachɛl, ka ra chɛɛhgi.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Denii u ŋɔ u be lugɔ u ɛɛ u mɛɛ lullii, di kaa ɛɛ Ribɛka ge biibaal. Awia, Rachɛl faa chɔ manaŋ di ga kali u mɛɛ.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Laban n wa nuu u naara Jekɔb wia, u hahzi manaŋ di lii chem u ni, di koruu di jaamuu, ka lawuu ba dia. Baŋ ballɛ la, Jekɔb jaa bagli gunla maa n du de tɛu.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Denii Laban ŋɔ ni bɔsɔna, “N challɛ hiŋ.” Jekɔb du de baagu bir. Jekɔb n kpa Lia ni Rachɛl wia

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: A hɔl bɛl, Laban ŋɔ u be, “A waa dɛkkɛ hiŋ kuu yaa n ɛɛ lulla wia di hum ra tɛŋ pɛɛpɛɛ. Awia, aŋmɛnɛɛ i ra nyiŋŋi n na turgiinei?”

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Lia siwee faa wesso. Ama, Rachɛl waarɛ faa mawɛɛ nɛnnɛ, di kaa ɛɛ toloori.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Jekɔb n faa ra nyiŋŋi Rachɛl wia la, u ŋɔ, “Di i daŋ kpa Rachɛl di tɛŋ, n daŋ hum tɛi binni aniidaanɛɛ.”

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Laban sɛɛ di ŋɔ, “Miŋ kpau tɛi bɔmbɔ, miŋ kpau tɛɛ nɛbɛl. Awia, so n be ɛŋ.”

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Denii Jekɔb faa jaa hum binni aniidaanɛɛ di tɛɛ Laban, ka ú kpa Rachɛl. Dikuu un faa ra nyiŋŋuu werge la wia, u binni lugɔ a saŋa faa waa nɛɛ u be.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: A hɔpizii n wa helle ú kpau la, u ŋɔ Laban be, “Filaŋkeŋwaa, n tuŋ n humaa. Awia, kpa n haaŋ tɛŋ.”

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Laban daali haŋfalli diuzenaa, di yir nɛr maa ba ba wa di.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Ka bɔr n wa biree, Laban lau Lia ga tɛɛ Jekɔb u nuu chɔɔ.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Laban lau u yoŋhaaŋ Zilpa tɛɛ Lia, ú ɛɛ u balii.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ka hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ, Jekɔb ŋɔ u binni di Liarɛ u nuu chɔɔ la. U ŋɔ Laban be, “Beedee wideere i ɛɛŋ kennei? N kuu Rachɛl wiarɛ di humii, ka ɛrɛɛ i jaa dariŋ kennei?”

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Denii Laban ŋɔ, “Yaa naabaala wiirɛ lugɔ a waa dɛkkɛ yaa n felee kpa yaa biihambijiga tɛɛ baal, ka ka u kpɛɛma.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Awia, di kanyitti, di i daŋ kaa sɛɛ hum binni aniidaanɛɛ jaa, n daŋ kpa u ge gba jaai. Di Lia haŋfalwia wa tunnɔ, yawa nua wa bɛri, i daŋ wer kpa Rachɛl ge jaa.”

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Jekɔb ni Rachɛl ge faa kaa chɔɔ. U ra nyiŋŋuu werge maa di kaali Lia. Aŋ ɛɛ gunla, u faa sɛɛ di hum a binni aniidaanɛɛ di jaa. Jekɔb biizi wia

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Kɔraawiizi n wa nawɛɛ lugɔ Jekɔb waa Lia ra nyiŋŋi nɛnnɛ la wia, u tɛu lulaa, ka Rachɛl waa haa waa wette ú lul.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Lia n kpogli hia la, u lul biibaal, ba dau u sɔŋ, Rubin. U faa ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, “Kɔraawiizi na n biinichɔgrɛ. N jaari daŋ jaa nɔŋŋi n ge.”

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Tama n wa jaawɛ, u kaa kpogli hia di kaa lul biibaal, ba dau u ge sɔŋ Simɛɔn. U kaa ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi nuuwɔɔ n jaari n wan nɔŋŋɛɛ, u kaa tɛŋ biibaal jaa.”

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: Uŋ wa lul nɛɛhiŋ la, u kaa kuari kpogli hia di kaa lul biibaal, Jekɔb dau u ge sɔŋ, Levi. Uŋ wa luluu la, u jaa ŋɔ, “Filaŋkeŋwaa, n jaari daŋ jaa nɔŋŋiŋ, dikuu miŋ lul biibaala bahoro tɛu wia.”

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Rachɛl n wa nawɛɛ lugɔ waa biizi ra lul la, u kpɛɛma Lia wia jaa ra wii u ni werge maa, u ŋɔ u jaari Jekɔb be, “Di hàá wer gil n lul bie, guŋwaa n daŋ siu.”

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Uŋ wa ŋɔ gunla, Jekɔb baaŋ fuuh, u pɛɛzuu, “Minuu Kɔraawiiziaa? Waabuu tɔ i lulaa.”

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Denii Rachɛl ŋɔ u be, “Guŋwaa, gil i ni n balii Bilha chɔɔ. Aŋhinabe, u daŋ wer lul biizi tɛŋ.”

ref: Then Rachel let Jacob marry Bilhah,
text: Awia, u kpa Bilha tɛɛ u jaari, u nuu chɔɔ.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Bilha kpogli hia di lul biibaal.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Rachɛl dau u sɔŋ, Dan. U faa ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, “Kɔraawiizi lizimbuu hiihzi biini, di nuu n wiisuŋŋaa, di tɛŋ biibaal.”

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Denii Bilha kaa wa kpogli hia, di lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Rachɛl kaa dau u sɔŋ, Naftali. U kaa ŋɔ, “N ni n kpɛɛma ra naau dikuu biizi wia. Ka n tamɔɔ lugɔ minuu jaa ra tɔ see ani biizi lulaa.”

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Lia ge n wa nawɛɛ lugɔ waa kaa ra kpogli hia la, u ge lau u balii Zilpa di tɛɛ u jaari, ú ɛɛ u haaŋ.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Denii Zilpa ge lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Lia ŋɔ, “N nyuu summɔɔ werge maa.” Awia, u dau u sɔŋ, Gad.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Lia kaa ŋɔ, “N dɔŋahina haana daŋ ŋɔ n nuu sumaa.” Awia, u dau u sɔŋ, Asɛ.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Mia kumaa saŋa, Rubin la koo di ga na daalunne la n na hiini haana ba ra lul, di kia ba wa tɛɛ u maa Lia. Denii Rachɛl suŋŋuu dí u tɛu aŋhina.

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Ka Lia na baaŋ di ŋɔ, “Hiŋ foozi n jaari la, i bɔra waa sɔɔnɛɛaa? I daŋ kaa wer foozi daalunne la n biibaal n wa tɛŋ la geaa?” Rachɛl ŋɔ, “Di i tɛŋ lunne naa, n daŋ gil i ni Jekɔb chɔɔ hamuŋa naa.”

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Awia, Jekɔb n wa lii koo di ra ba, Lia lii chem u ni, di ŋɔ, “N nii daŋ chɔɔ hamuŋa naa, dikuubɛɛwiai, n kpa n biibaal daalunnee turgi i kim tɛɛ Rachɛl.” Awia, u ni Jekɔb chɔɔ a hamuŋa hiŋ.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Kɔraawiizi nuu u wiisuŋŋaa, di gil u kpogli hia, di lul u biibambinuehiŋ tɛɛ Jekɔb.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Denii Lia ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi turgi n kinnɛ, dikuu miŋ kpa n balii tɛɛ n jaari u ɛɛ u haaŋ wia.” Awia, u dau u ge sɔŋ, Isaka.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Lia ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi tɛmbuu kunnanii. Filaŋkeŋ, n jaari danna tɔgziŋ, dikuu miŋ lul biibaala banumbɛl tɛu wia.” Awia, u dau u ge sɔŋ, Zɛbulɔn.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Saŋa bɛl n wa helle, u lul biihaaŋ, di dau u sɔŋ Daina.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: U kpogli hia di lul biibaal. Uŋ wa lullɛ la, u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi lizimbuu hiihzi biini.”

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: U jaa dau u sɔŋ Joosɛf, di ŋɔ, “Kɔ́raawiizi kaa jaambuu biibaal.” Jekɔb sela wazaa wia

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rachɛl n wa lul Joosɛf la, Jekɔb ŋɔ Laban be, “Taŋ ŋmɛnɛɛ ń bir kaali n bol.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Gil n kpogli n haana ni n biizi maa miŋ kuu ba wia di humii la, di ra la. I zum gunla miŋ humii maguu.”

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Jekɔb ŋɔ u be, “I zum gunla maa miŋ hummɛ tɛi maguu, di na gunla miŋ binni i sela ni a wazi werge maa.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Miŋ ballɛ, di a waa kpil. Ka filaŋkeŋ, i jaa ɛɛ dɔɔnnɛ werge maa. I himmaa zum lugɔ Kɔraawiizi kissiibuu werge maa, dikuu n wia. Filaŋkennaa, saŋa bɛɛ ń ge ɛɛ wii bɛl tɛɛ n diahinai?”

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Laban pɛɛzuu, “N turgii aŋmɛnɛi?” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Gilɛɛ tɛŋ kummaa. Ama, di i daŋ ɛɛ wikpaŋ naa tɛŋ, n daŋ haa ra binni i sela ni.

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Waabuu keŋ. Gil n binni i sela biini, di ligzi peeza la maa n chɛssɛɛ gɛɛ di lɛgzi, pebizi la n biree ani buuŋ la maa n chɛssɛɛ gɛɛ di lɛgzi, á ɛɛ n kinturgaa.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Gunnɛ niŋ gil i zum lugɔ wibii maa, n wia dɔɔ wibiire. Di i wa na buuŋ gɛɛ peezi maa n sela biini u ɛɛ u huŋŋu, u yemmaa waa lɛgzɛɛ, gɛɛ pebii maa n waa biree, amɔ, guŋwaa n ŋmɛɛubuu.”

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Denii Laban sɛɛ di ŋɔ ú ɛɛ gunla uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la.

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: A hɔl himmaanii Laban ligzi bubala la maa n lɛgzɛɛ gɛɛ di piizi, buniinazi la maa n chɛssɛɛ gɛɛ di lɛgzi ani pebibiŋŋe maa, di gil u biibaala ra binnaa.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: Denii u ni u biibaala nɔɔnaa di vol hɔpizaa ahoro di kaali bɔɔnɛɛ, ba waa kaa dugli Jekɔb. Ka Jekɔb waa haa ra binni Laban sela la n kawɛɛ la ni.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Denii Jekɔb teŋri daazi aŋhina kela, di piili a hɛga yeŋa, a daazi bɔhuŋŋa nyiizi,
