ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: di kpaa baŋzi bɔra la Laban sela la n na nyɔɔ nii. Di a sela wa ballɔ á wa nyɔɔ nii la saŋaa a ra deezi.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Jekɔb ɔɔ bargi peeza la di lii buuna la ni, di gil a siwee chaŋ Laban hora la n lɛgzɛɛ di biri la. Gunnɛ u ɛɛ di na u himmaa peeza, di lii Laban hora biini.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: A seniinazi la n kpekke la haŋ wa ŋɔ a deezi maa, Jekɔb ɔɔ kpa daalɛgzaa labuu di baŋzi a see.

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Ama, u faa waa ɔɔ ra ɛɛ a sela la waa n gɛrgɛɛ la guŋ. Awia, anla n gɛrgɛɛ la birgi Laban hora, ka waabuu hora ɛɛ a kpega.

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Uŋ faa ɛɛ gunla, guŋ gil u dɔɔ sela werge maa, di birgi dɔɔŋ, di dɔɔ yoma, nyugmazi ani kauzi werge maa.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Jekɔb nuu Laban biibaala n na ŋɔ, “Jekɔb fini yaa mɛɛrɛ, di lɛɛ u kundɔɔlaa maa. Yaa mɛɛ benii Jekɔb na u daama maa.”

ref: Jacob also noticed that Laban was not as friendly as he had been before.
text: Jekɔb jaa na lugɔ u ni Laban waa kaa du sum.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “Izi, di bir la i mɛɛ waa hɛri, i lulla be. N daŋ du i be.”

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Jekɔb yir Rachɛl ni Lia, bá ba bɔr la unna binni u sela la.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Denii u ŋɔ ba be, “Filaŋkennaa, n ni ii mɛɛ waa kaa du sum. Ka Kɔraawiizi la n mɛɛ n na hum la waa du n benii.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Ií zum gunla miŋ hummɛ ni n fɔŋŋa maanii,

ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: ka u finimbuu di lau n kinturgaa birgi bufi. Ama, Kɔraawiizi waa sɛɛwɛ ú ɛɛŋ wichɔgii maa.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Di u haŋ wa ŋɔ, ‘Sela la maa n niŋ lɛgzi daŋ ɛɛ i kinturgaa,’ a sela maa ɔɔ wa lul a lɛgzaarɛ, di u kaa wa ŋɔ, ‘A piizaa maa daŋ ɛɛ i kinturgaa,’ a sela maa ɔɔ kaa ra lul a piizaarɛ.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: A sela deezaa saŋa n wa helle la, n chɔɔwɔɔ di deezi di na lugɔ bubala la n na deezaa la maa piizɔɔrɛ, di chɛssi gɛɛ di lɛgzi.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: A deeza biini, Kɔraawiizi mɛlika yiriŋ, ‘Jekɔb!’ N sɛɛ, di ŋɔ, ‘Minuu keŋ.’

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: U ŋɔ n be, ‘Na, bubala la maa n na deezaa la piizɔɔrɛ, di chɛssi gɛɛ di lɛgzi, dikuubɛɛwiai, n na gunla maa Laban n na ɛɛibuu.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Minuu Kɔraawiizi la n faa ba i be Bɛtɛl, bɔr la hiŋ ɛɛ ɔlivi nuu di waari bii bɛl ni, ka ŋɔ nua tɛŋ labuu. Awia, ɛɛ sirya di bir kaali hɛri la baŋ lulii la.’ ”

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: U kpogli yaabuu alugɔ hɔɔhra. Waa yaa ra ɛɛ lugɔ u himmaa biizi. U yoo yaabuu, di di kebee la maa baŋ turguu la.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: Daama la maa Kɔraawiizi n lɛɛ yaa mɛɛ be di tɛi la, ɛɛ yaa ni yaa biizi horaa. Awia, ɛɛ gunla maa Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔ i be la.”

ref: Then Jacob, his wives, and his children got on camels and left
text: Denii Jekɔb gil u haana ni u biizi maa tinni nyugmazi.

ref: northern Syria for the home of his father Isaac in Canaan. Jacob took along all his flocks, herds, and other property.
text: U duŋŋi sela la maa, di kpa kuna la un nawɛɛ Mɛsɔpɔtemia la, di tɔ see, ú la u mɛɛ Aizik be, Keenan hɛri.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: A saŋa, di Laban laŋŋɔ ú ga fuŋ u peeza huna. Rachɛl ge ŋmɛɛ u mɛɛ dia vuga.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jekɔb faa dar Laban, waa sɛɛwɛ u zum lugɔ u ra chɔu.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: Jekɔb kpa u daama la maa di lii chɔ, ba ga gi Muu Yufretis, di ra la Giliad giŋgimee hɛri. Laban n dau Jekɔb luge wia

ref: Three days later Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
text: Ba kaalaa hɔpizaa ahoro hɔllɛ, ba ŋɔ Laban be lugɔ Jekɔb chɔwɔɔ.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Denii u lau u lulla di ra duŋŋi Jekɔb luge. Ba duŋŋi u luge guŋŋ hɔpizaa aniidaanɛɛ, di ga huuzuu Giliad giŋgimee hɛri.

ref: But God appeared to Laban in a dream that night and warned, “Don't say a word to Jacob. Don't make a threat or a promise.”
text: A hamuŋa ni, Kɔraawiizi ba Laban be deeza biini, di wa ŋɔ, “A du tau, di gilɛɛ waa ŋɔ winanaa gɛɛ wichɔgaa maa di ra tɛɛ Jekɔb.”

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Laban n wa huuzi Jekɔb, di u baŋ u tanti diini Giliad giŋgimee hɛri la, u ge chɔɔ de.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Laban ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “Bɛɛ i ɛɛ gunnɛi? I darimbuu di lauri n biihaana lugɔ baŋ lauri nɛra laal biini, di ra chɔ.

ref: Why did you sneak away without telling me? I would have given you a going-away party with singing and with music on tambourines and harps.
text: Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i suahzi di ra chɔi? N daŋ vii gil nɛra ba, di wa yigzi kuna, di lɔ kemzi, di yir yira di sa ni bɔsɔna mɔɔ, ka i vii haa lii.

ref: You didn't even give me a chance to kiss my own grandchildren and daughters goodbye. That was really foolish.
text: Haa gillɛ n faami n biihaana ni n naanna, ka ta baa ŋmɛnɛɛ. Gɛnni wiarɛ i ɛɛ la.

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: N zummɔ lugɔ i bɔra ra nyurguu i la bol, ka ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i kaa ŋmɛɛ n dia vugai?”

ref: Jacob answered, “I left secretly because I was afraid you would take your daughters from me by force.
text: Denii Jekɔb ŋɔ, “N hahzoore lii, dikuubɛɛwiai, n faa ra ɔmuu lugɔ i daŋ foozi i biihaana.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Di i wa na i vuga nɛr maa be, kpu a hiŋ. N kaa ra ŋɔ i benii yaa lulla naa see lugɔ í himmaa pigzi na, di i wa na i kummaa n be, í kpa.” Jekɔb n ŋɔ gunla, di waa zummɛ lugɔ Rachɛllɛ ŋmɛɛ a vuga.

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Awia, Laban feli zuu Jekɔb tanti dia di pigzuu, di kaa lii zuu Lia ni ba balaa banɛɛ la ge diini, waa kummaa nawɛɛ. Denii u kaa zuu Rachɛl ge tanti dia.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Rachɛllɛ faa ŋmɛɛ a vuga, di kpaa du bɔɔti biini, di du u nyugma kara zɛɛ di so a ni. Laban n wa zuu u tanti dia la, Rachɛl tinni so a ni. Uŋ faa jaa ɛɛ gunla, Laban pigzi a tanti dia maa, ka wàá nawɛɛ.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Denii Rachɛl ŋɔ, “N mɛ, gilɛɛ waa na baaŋ, miŋ waa wette izi chi i see la, n na ɛɛ doŋaa.” Awia, Laban faa pigzi a tanti dia maa, ka waa u dia vuga la nawɛɛ.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Denii Jekɔb baaŋ fuuh, u pɛɛzi Laban, “Bɛɛ wichɔgiirɛ n ɛɛinɛi, i ra duŋŋi n lugei?

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Hiŋ binni n kuna maa biini, bɛɛ i na u ɛɛ i horei? Kunla hiŋ nawɛɛ u ɛɛ i kuŋ, kpau lii yaa lulla naa maa see bá na, di zum unla hor n summɛ.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Haali di sechɔgii wa lau bɛl maa, an turgi u kinnɛ. Maa ɔɔ kpau ba i na gba. I ɔɔ giluu n turgi unla maa baŋ ŋmɛɛwɛ, haali di hamuŋaa gɛɛ di wibaŋa gbaa.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Wiizi ɔɔ kpumbuu wibaŋa ni, waari ge kpuŋ hamuŋa, maa ɔɔ wer n chɔ dɔɔ.

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Na, di Kɔraawiizi la n mɛɛ Aizik n na hum ani u ge mɛɛ Abraham n faa ra hum la wia lɛi, i daŋ vii duŋŋiŋ ta, di haŋ tɛɛwɛ kummaa. Ama, Kɔraawiizi zum tɔɔrɔɔ la maa miŋ diweere, di na gunla maa miŋ kuuhwɛ. Awiarɛ u hau i ni, dɛɛla hamuŋa ni la.” Jekɔb ni Laban nuukpaŋ wia

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Laban n wa nuu guŋ, u ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “Haana naa ɛɛ n biihaanaa, biizi naa ge ɛɛ n biizi, sela naa ɛɛ n sela. Kuna naa maa hiŋ dɔɔwɛ naa ɛɛ n horaa. Ka ɛrɛɛ ń ɛɛ zinaa, dikuu n biihaana naa ni biizi la baŋ lullɛ wiai?

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: Awia, gil n nii ɛɛ nuukpaŋ filaŋkeŋ, ú ɛɛ daansɛɛ kuŋ yaa baaŋ.”

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Denii Jekɔb kpa bii bɛl di baŋ,

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: ka ŋɔ u lulla be, “Ií chaali bia dau kululu.” Baŋ wa ɛɛ guŋ tuŋ, Jekɔb ni Laban maa so a bia zɛɛ di di.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Laban faa dau de sɔŋ, Jɛga Sahaduta, Jekɔb ge dau de sɔŋ, Galiid.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “This pile of rocks will remind us of our agreement.” That's why the place was named Galeed.
text: Laban ŋɔ, “A pii naabuu niŋ ɛɛ yaa nuukpaŋ dɔɔi naa daansɛɛ kuŋ.”

ref: Laban also said, “This pile of rocks means that the Lord will watch us both while we are apart from each other.” So the place was also named Mizpah.
text: Ba faa kaa ra yir de, Mizpa, dikuubɛɛwiai, Laban faa ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, “Kɔ́raawiizi ra du yaa maa tau, di yaa wa bargi dɔŋa be.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Di i waa tɔɔri n biihaana naa gɛɛ di kaa kpa haana baŋhina jaa, haali nɛr maa n waa hoo yaa be naa gba, a liizi lugɔ Kɔraawiizii ɛɛ yaa daansɛɛ hiŋ.”

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Pii naa ni bii naa ɛɛ daansɛɛ kunaa. Bɛl maa wàá ka bɛri a ni, di ga ɛɛ u dɔŋ wichɔgii maa.”

ref: My father Nahor, your grandfather Abraham, and their ancestors all worshiped the same God, and he will make sure that we each keep the agreement. Then Jacob made a promise in the name of the fearsome God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: Laban kaa ŋɔ, “N na jaami Kɔraawiizi la i naabaal Abraham n faa ra hum la ni unla u mɛɛ Nahɔ n faa ra hum labuu, lugɔ ú tɔɔri unla maa n niŋ wa kpa wichɔgii maa ɛɛ u dɔŋhiŋ.” Denii Jekɔb hɛɛhzi Kɔraawiizi la u mɛɛ Aizik n na hum la lugɔ u danna hiŋ a nua.

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: U kaa kpu sel di lizi mbusu tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi a giŋgimee hɛri, di yir u lulla bá ba wa di. Baŋ wa diwee tuŋ, ba maa chɔɔ de a hamuŋa ni.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Hɔɔŋ waa pizi chɔɔzimpikke ni, Laban izi di faami u naanna ni u biihaana, ka dibi tɛɛ baa, ka bir kpo.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb ni u diahina maa n kaa kpa ŋmɛnɛɛ di ra kaali la, Kɔraawiizi mɛlikazi wa chem ba ni.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: A guŋ saŋa hiŋ, Jekɔb faa lizi balaa baŋhina, bá feli la u kpɛɛma Ɛsau be, Ɛdɔm hɛri.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Denii u ŋɔ ba be, “Ií la ga ŋɔ n dɔɔri Ɛsau be, dí u balii Jekɔb la n du u naara Laban be di birgi la ra jaamuu werge maa.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: U dɔɔ nauna, kauzi, peeza, buuna ani balaa kpila maanii filaŋkeŋ. Awiarɛ u tili ŋɔ ya wa kalii lugɔ u ra bau. Ka u ra suŋŋiibuu lugɔ í lɛɛu ani bɔsɔna.”

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: A huŋhunna bir ba Jekɔb be di wa ŋɔ, “Yaa la i kpɛɛma Ɛsau benii. U ra bau di kpogli baala kalfaanaazu bá wa chem i ni.”

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Jekɔb n wa nuu guŋ, ɔnsii ni biinichɔg lawuu, u lau u diahina ni u peeza, nauna ani u nyugmazi la maa bɔnni bɔra anɛɛ.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: U faa fɛwɔɔrɛ lugɔ di Ɛsau ni u laallɔɔra la wa kpegli bɔkpaŋ, ka baŋhina waa wer chɔ di lɛɛ bahinaa.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Denii Jekɔb kaa suŋŋi wiizi di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la n naabaal Abraham ni n mɛɛ Aizik n faa ra hum, hinuu ŋɔ n be, ‘Bir la i himmaa hɛri ni i nɛra be, n daŋ gil i nyɛɛli.’

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: N na suŋŋiibuu, lɛɛŋ di lii n kpɛɛma Ɛsau be. N na ɔmuu lugɔ u daŋ wa kpu n ni n haana ni biizi maa.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ka i ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, ‘N daŋ gil i nyɛɛli wibii maa, di gil ń naanna wazi di du kpil alugɔ muuzeŋ hɛɛhriichaa baŋ wàá wer kur la.’ ”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Denii Jekɔb chɔɔ bɔr la un duwɛɛ la, hɔpizi u lizi u sela aŋhina ú kpa ga sunzi Ɛsau.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Dí ba bɛl maa ge ɔɔ liizi di ŋɔ lugɔ Jekɔb du harbɔɔ di ra ba. U ra fɛu lugɔ sela la unna sunzi Ɛsau la daŋ wer bir Ɛsau nfɛrɛ mɔɔ, ka u haa wa chem u ni, ú vii lɛɛ baa ani bɔsɔna.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Uŋ wa kissuu tuŋ, Jekɔb dau de sɔŋ Pɛniɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, u na Kɔraawiizii siwii ni, ka waa siuwɛ.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: U gil a sela maa du a sugezi, ú balaa hiŋ bɛl ra binni a suge bɛl maa ni. U ŋɔ ba be, “Ií tɔ see di ra vol, di gilɛɛ ra dugli dɔŋa.”

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Denii Jekɔb kaa ŋɔ u balii la n tɔ see la be, “Di i wa chem n kpɛɛma Ɛsau ni u pɛɛzii, lee i ra lanɛi? Anɛɛ baliirɛ hinnɛi? Anɛɛ selaa tɔ i see maa kennei?

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ŋɔ u be lugɔ u balii Jekɔb kunaa u kpa di ra ba ú wa tɛɛ u dɔɔri Ɛsau. U haa du harbɔɔ di ra ba.”

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Gunnɛ Jekɔb faa ŋɔ tɛɛ a senɔɔna sugesugezi la maa di wa kaali, di karfi baa lugɔ di ba wa chem Ɛsau ni, ba maa ŋɔ wikpaŋ la uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la tɛu.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Gunnɛ u faa ɛɛ di gil ba kpa a kunsunzaa tɔ see, ka waabuu himmaa faa haa chɔɔ ba chɔɔribɔr hiŋ a hamuŋa ni. Jekɔb ni mɛlika n yoowe wia

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Uŋ wa gillɛ ba gi a muu la, u gil ba jaa paa u kuna maa ge di gi.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Awia, Jekɔb bɛŋ jaa ka de. Denii baal bɛl ba u be, u nuu yoo guŋŋ di ra ba hɔpizaa.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: A baal n wa binnɛɛ lugɔ wàá wer kpegli Jekɔb la, u lɔ u kɛra, u kpuzi.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Denii a baal ŋɔ, “Gil n kaali, bɔr chaanɔɔrɛ.” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Màá gil i kaali, di haŋ kissee lɛi.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Denii a baal pɛɛzuu, “I sɔnnɛ ɛrɛɛ?” U ŋɔ, “N sɔnnɛ Jekɔb.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Filaŋkeŋ i sɔŋ waa kaa di Jekɔb. Ba daŋ jaa ra yirii Izrɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, i ni Kɔraawiizi ani baala yoowoo, ka ba waa wette bá kpeglii.”

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Jekɔb ge bir pɛɛzuu, “Ka i ge sɔnnɛ ɛrɛɛ?” A baal ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i ra nyiŋŋi i zum n sɔnɛi?” Denii u kissuu.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Wiizi n wa futtɛ, u lii a Pɛniɛl di kaa ra kaali. U faa ra dugzuu dikuu a baal n lɔ u kɛra la wia.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Haali ni zinaa, Izrɛl hina waa sɛɛwɛ ra hee sel maa kɛra, dikuu uŋ lɔ Jekɔb kɛra wia.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Denii Jekɔb kaa suŋŋi wiizi di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la n naabaal Abraham ni n mɛɛ Aizik n faa ra hum, hinuu ŋɔ n be, ‘Bir la i himmaa hɛri ni i nɛra be, n daŋ gil i nyɛɛli.’

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: N na suŋŋiibuu, lɛɛŋ di lii n kpɛɛma Ɛsau be. N na ɔmuu lugɔ u daŋ wa kpu n ni n haana ni biizi maa.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ka i ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, ‘N daŋ gil i nyɛɛli wibii maa, di gil ń naanna wazi di du kpil alugɔ muuzeŋ hɛɛhriichaa baŋ wàá wer kur la.’ ”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Denii Jekɔb chɔɔ bɔr la un duwɛɛ la, hɔpizi u lizi u sela aŋhina ú kpa ga sunzi Ɛsau.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Dí ba bɛl maa ge ɔɔ liizi di ŋɔ lugɔ Jekɔb du harbɔɔ di ra ba. U ra fɛu lugɔ sela la unna sunzi Ɛsau la daŋ wer bir Ɛsau nfɛrɛ mɔɔ, ka u haa wa chem u ni, ú vii lɛɛ baa ani bɔsɔna.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Uŋ wa kissuu tuŋ, Jekɔb dau de sɔŋ Pɛniɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, u na Kɔraawiizii siwii ni, ka waa siuwɛ.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: U gil a sela maa du a sugezi, ú balaa hiŋ bɛl ra binni a suge bɛl maa ni. U ŋɔ ba be, “Ií tɔ see di ra vol, di gilɛɛ ra dugli dɔŋa.”

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Denii Jekɔb kaa ŋɔ u balii la n tɔ see la be, “Di i wa chem n kpɛɛma Ɛsau ni u pɛɛzii, lee i ra lanɛi? Anɛɛ baliirɛ hinnɛi? Anɛɛ selaa tɔ i see maa kennei?

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ŋɔ u be lugɔ u balii Jekɔb kunaa u kpa di ra ba ú wa tɛɛ u dɔɔri Ɛsau. U haa du harbɔɔ di ra ba.”

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Gunnɛ Jekɔb faa ŋɔ tɛɛ a senɔɔna sugesugezi la maa di wa kaali, di karfi baa lugɔ di ba wa chem Ɛsau ni, ba maa ŋɔ wikpaŋ la uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la tɛu.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Gunnɛ u faa ɛɛ di gil ba kpa a kunsunzaa tɔ see, ka waabuu himmaa faa haa chɔɔ ba chɔɔribɔr hiŋ a hamuŋa ni. Jekɔb ni mɛlika n yoowe wia

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Uŋ wa gillɛ ba gi a muu la, u gil ba jaa paa u kuna maa ge di gi.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Awia, Jekɔb bɛŋ jaa ka de. Denii baal bɛl ba u be, u nuu yoo guŋŋ di ra ba hɔpizaa.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: A baal n wa binnɛɛ lugɔ wàá wer kpegli Jekɔb la, u lɔ u kɛra, u kpuzi.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Denii a baal ŋɔ, “Gil n kaali, bɔr chaanɔɔrɛ.” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Màá gil i kaali, di haŋ kissee lɛi.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Denii a baal pɛɛzuu, “I sɔnnɛ ɛrɛɛ?” U ŋɔ, “N sɔnnɛ Jekɔb.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Filaŋkeŋ i sɔŋ waa kaa di Jekɔb. Ba daŋ jaa ra yirii Izrɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, i ni Kɔraawiizi ani baala yoowoo, ka ba waa wette bá kpeglii.”

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Jekɔb ge bir pɛɛzuu, “Ka i ge sɔnnɛ ɛrɛɛ?” A baal ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ wiarɛ i ra nyiŋŋi i zum n sɔnɛi?” Denii u kissuu.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Wiizi n wa futtɛ, u lii a Pɛniɛl di kaa ra kaali. U faa ra dugzuu dikuu a baal n lɔ u kɛra la wia.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Haali ni zinaa, Izrɛl hina waa sɛɛwɛ ra hee sel maa kɛra, dikuu uŋ lɔ Jekɔb kɛra wia.

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: di gil u hahjigzi banɛɛ la ni ba biizi tɔ see, Lia ge ni u biizi hiŋ, ka Rachɛl ni Joosɛf jaa tɔ har.

ref: Jacob himself walked in front of them all, bowing to the ground seven times as he came near his brother.
text: Denii Jekɔb jaa tɔ see di ra chem u kpɛɛma ni. Unna chem u ni la, u tognuu naa buniidaanɛɛ.

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Ka Ɛsau chɔ di chem u ni, di faami u baŋaa, ka suuzuu. Ba wii du dɔŋa.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Denii Ɛsau binni di na haana la ni ba biizi la, di pɛɛzi, “Lee hinaa kennei?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Biizi naabuu Kɔraawiizi suŋ i balii ni, di tɛu.”

ref: Then the two servant women and their children came and bowed down to Esau.
text: Denii u hahjigzi la ni ba biizi feli wa togni jaamuu.

ref: Next, Leah and her children came and bowed down; finally, Joseph and Rachel also came and bowed down.
text: Lia ge ni u biizi hiŋ wa togni jaamuu mɔɔ ka Joosɛf ni Rachɛl ge wa togni jaamuu.

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Ɛsau kaa pɛɛzi, “Ka suge la miŋ chem u ni lau? U ge zɛɛrɛ ɛrɛɛ?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “I sunzaarɛ n kpa ra ba ń wa tɛi, ka í lɛɛŋ ani bɔsɔna.”

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Ka Ɛsau bir ŋɔ, “N niwee, n dɔɔ aŋhinaa werge. Awia, kpogli i kuna.”

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Denii Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Akai, di n wia parii, amɔ, í lɛɛ a kuna. I nai ɛɛwɔɔ lugɔ miŋ na Kɔraawiizi, dikuu hiŋ lɛɛŋ ni biinihuŋŋu wia.

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.
text: Jaande maa, lɛɛ n sunzaa naa, dikuubɛɛwiai, Kɔraawiizi nɔŋŋimbuu di tɛŋ kunla maa minna nyiŋŋi.” Dikuu Jekɔb haa ra suŋŋuu werge maa la wia, u jaa wa sɛɛ di lɛɛ a kuna.

ref: “Let's get ready to travel,” Esau said. “I'll go along with you.”
text: Ɛsau n wa lɛɛa la, u ŋɔ, “Too, guŋwaa, ya ra la, n daŋ tɔ i see.”

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Denii Jekɔb bir ŋɔ u be, “N dɔɔri, n biizi naa baŋhina haa du jigzii, n peeza ni nauna gba aŋhina dɔɔ biifullii. Di i ra duŋŋaa kpegzi ni, hɔpizikpaŋ gba, a daŋ siuzi.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Awia, tɔ see di ra la, ka ya kpogli a biizi ni a sela bɔɔiih di ra ba. Gunla maa, yaa daŋ wa huuzii Ɛdɔm.”

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Ɛsau ŋɔ, “Guŋwaa, n daŋ gil n baala naa baŋhina i be.” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Akai, a kukkɔɔrɛ. N dɔɔri, haa gil n wia parii.”

ref: So Esau left for Edom.
text: Awia, a hɔl hiŋ, Ɛsau kaa bir kaali Ɛdɔm.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Ka Jekɔb waa ni u balaa faa vol di wa hel Sukɔf, u ma u dia ka du pampannizi di tɛɛ u sela ge. Awia, u dau de sɔŋ Sukɔf.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Jekɔb n faa lii Mɛsɔpɔtemia la, u wa hel bolzeŋ Sɛkɛm n du Keenan hɛri labuu ni bɔsɔna, di chɔɔ dugli a bolzeŋ.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Jekɔb n faa chɔɔ de la, u kpa saliŋhuŋŋa kebisau kalfa, di yau a hɛri lii Sɛkɛm mɛɛ Hamɔ naanna be.

ref: then he set up his tents and built an altar there to honor the God of Israel.
text: Denii u faa kuari mbusu lizibɔr de, di ra yir de Ɛl Ɛlɔhi Izrɛl.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Denii a hɔl bɛl Lia ni Jekɔb biihaaŋ Daina izi di la ú ga jaami a hɛri haana.

ref: She was seen by Hamor's son Shechem, the leader of the Hivites, and he grabbed her and raped her.
text: Hamɔ la n ɛɛ Hivi hinuu, di ɛɛ de koori biibaal Sɛkɛm nau, di pumuu.

ref: But Shechem was attracted to Dinah, so he told her how much he loved her.
text: Sɛkɛm bampirii faa jaa lau Daina, u ra nyiŋŋuu, di ra fɔrguu.

ref: Shechem even asked his father to arrange for him to marry her.
text: Haali u ŋɔ u mɛɛ be, dí u nyiŋŋuu tɛu, ú kpa.

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Jekɔb n wa nuuwɛ lugɔ ba chɔgi u biihaaŋ Daina, di u biibaala faa haa du pir di ra binni ba sela. Awia, u faa siigi ka ra kpaŋ ba balaa.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: Jekɔb biibambizi n wa nuu guŋ, ba lii pir di ba. Ba biine chɔgi werge, ba baana ra fuuh werge maa, dikuu gunla Sɛkɛm n pum Jekɔb biihaaŋ Daina, di du Izrɛl hina hiihzi wia, dikuubɛɛwiai, guŋ waa dɛkkɛ.

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Denii Hamɔ ŋɔ Jekɔb ni u biibaala be, “N biibaal Sɛkɛm ra nyiŋŋi i biihaaŋ labuu werge maa. Awia, ii gil u kpau.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Ií gil ya ɛɛ nuukpaŋ, ii biizi yuu yaa biizi, yaa ge biizi paagi ii ge biizi.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Di guŋ ɛɛwɔ, i daŋ wer du yaa be, yaa hɛri naa bɔr la maa hinna nyiŋŋi, di ra di soochi, di dɔɔ kuna.”

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Denii Sɛkɛm jaa suŋŋi Jekɔb ni u biibaala, di ŋɔ, “Di ii sɛɛwɔ n kpa Daina, n daŋ tɛɛ ia kunla maa iinna nyiŋŋi.

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Ií bagliŋ kunla iinna nyiŋŋi, haali di a kim mag gunla maa, ka n daŋ turgi, di ii daŋ sɛɛ ń kpau.”

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Jekɔb biibaala faa ŋɔ wiarɛ di dar Sɛkɛm ni u mɛɛ Hamɔ, dikuu gunla Sɛkɛm n chɔgi ba niuhambie Daina la wia.

ref: So they tricked them by saying: You're not circumcised! It would be a disgrace for us to let you marry Dinah now.
text: Awia, ba faa ŋɔ Sɛkɛm ni u mɛɛ Hamɔ be, “Yaa wàá sɛɛ, dikuubɛɛwiai, Sɛkɛm waa u hɛŋ teŋŋe. A daŋ ɛɛ yaa hiihzi werge maa lugɔ Daina yuu baal la n waa u hɛŋ teŋŋe.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Yaa daŋ sɛɛ, di ii sɛɛwɔ lugɔ ii du yaa duula, di teŋri ii biibaala maa hɛna.

ref: Then your families can marry into ours, and ours can marry into yours, and we can live together like one nation.
text: Di ii wetto ɛɛ guŋ, yaa daŋ wer ra kpa dɔŋa biihaana, di so dɔŋa be, di ɛɛ kpaŋ.

ref: But if you don't agree to be circumcised, we'll take Dinah and leave this place.
text: Di ii waa sɛɛ teŋri i hɛna waa, yaa daŋ kpa yaa niuhambie kaali.”

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Sɛkɛm hahzi manaŋ di ɛɛ gunla maa baŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la, dikuubɛɛwiai, u faa ra nyiŋŋi Dainaa ni u bampirii maa. Di faa kaa ɛɛ nɛr la u lulla maa n na tɔgzi ge.

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:
text: Guŋ wia, u ni u mɛɛ faa la a bol nabɔnzi be a bol dominuuzeŋ, di ga lizi amaniye tɛɛ baa.

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: Ba ŋɔ, “Nɛra naa wia wessoo. Awia, ií gil bá so a hɛri di ra di soochi. A hɛri kannɔɔ lugɔ ba daŋ wer so u biini. Yaa daŋ wer di ra paagi ba biihaana, bá ge ra paagi yaa hora.

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Ama, ba wàá sɛɛ so yaa be, yaa ni baa ɛɛ kpaŋ, di yaa waa yaa hɛna teŋree alugɔ baŋ teŋri ba hora la.

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: Ií na, di yaa sɛɛwɔ di teŋri yaa hɛna, ba sela naa ni ba kundɔɔlaa naa maa daŋ birgi yaa hora. Awia, ii gil ya sɛɛ guŋ, bá so yaa be.”

ref: Every grown man followed this advice and got circumcised. Dinah's Brothers Take Revenge
text: Denii a bolzeŋ nɛra maa sɛɛ gunla Hamɔ ni u biibaal Sɛkɛm n ŋɔwɛɛ la. A baala maa jaa teŋri ba hɛna.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Aŋ wa ɛɛ hɔpizaa ahoro, di ba bɔra haa ra wii baa, Jekɔb biibaala banɛɛ, Simɛɔn ni Levi n ɛɛ Daina niubambizi, kpa ba tokobizi di zuu a bolzeŋ di pig baa, di kpuuzi ba baala maa nyaamm.

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.
text: Jekɔb biizi la n kawɛɛ la maa faa izi, di gbaari a bol kummaa, dikuu baŋ pum ba niuhambie de, di chɔguu la wia.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Ba lauri ba peeza, ba nauna, ba kauzi, di paagi kunla maa baŋ nawɛɛ a bolzeŋ ni ba kooni biine maa.

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: Ba paagi ba daama, di lauri ba haana ni ba biizi ani ba diini kuna maa.

ref: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “Look what you've done! Now I'm in real trouble with the Canaanites and Perizzites who live around here. There aren't many of us, and if they attack, they'll kill everyone in my household.”
text: Jekɔb ŋɔ Levi ni Simɛɔn be, “Ií nyiŋŋi wiarɛ tɛŋ la. Keenan hina ni a Pɛriz hina n du hɛri naa biini maa, daŋ jaa kpaŋ birgi ba dɔm. Yaa waa kpil. Di ba izoo ɛɛ nuukpaŋ di ŋɔ ba yoo yaa, ba daŋ kpuuzi yaa nyaamm.”

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ, ba ŋɔ, “Amɔ, a dɛkkɔ uŋ ɛɛ yaa niuhambie lugɔ sɛnsɛnniaa?”

ref: God told Jacob, “Return to Bethel, where I appeared to you when you were running from your brother Esau. Make your home there and build an altar for me.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi faa ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “Izi la Bɛtɛl di ga so de. Di i wa la de, kuari mbusu lizibɔr tɛɛ mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi la n faa ba i be, saŋa la hiŋ chɔwɛɛ lii i kpɛɛma Ɛsau be la.”

ref: Jacob said to his family and to everyone else who was traveling with him: Get rid of your foreign gods! Then make yourselves acceptable to worship God and put on clean clothes.
text: Jekɔb ŋɔ u haana ni biizi ni nɛra la maa n du u be la be, “Ií kpa vuga la maa n du ii be la ha, ka sɔ iihinaa di lizi ii doŋa ha ka ɛɛ nɛnanaa Kɔraawiizi be, di kpa ii kunnanaa di laali.

ref: Afterwards, we'll go to Bethel. I will build an altar there for God, who answered my prayers when I was in trouble and who has always been at my side.
text: Yaa daŋ lii ɛŋ di la Bɛtɛl, ń ga ma mbusu lizibɔr tɛɛ Kɔraawiizi la n faa ɔɔ nuu n wiisuŋŋaa, saŋa la miŋ faa ɔɔ du tɔɔrɔɔ biini, u du n be bɔr la maa miŋ ɔɔ ra la la.”

ref: So everyone gave Jacob their idols and their earrings, and he buried them under the oak tree near Shechem.
text: Denii ba faa kpa ba vuga ni dignikuna maa baŋ dɔɔwɛ la tɛɛ Jekɔb. U faa kpaa hug daazeŋ bɛl zɛɛ n dugli Sɛkɛm.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Baŋ faa wa lii de la, Kɔraawiizi gil ɔnsii werge lau nɛra la n du bola la maa n bar baa la, ba faa waa sɛɛwɛ bá duŋŋi ba luge.

ref: Jacob built an altar there and called it “God of Bethel,” because that was the place where God had appeared to him when he was running from Esau.
text: Jekɔb faa jaa ma mbusu lizibɔr de, di dau de sɔŋ Ɛl Bɛtɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, denii Kɔraawiizi faa ba u be, saŋa la uŋ chɔwɛɛ di ra lii u kpɛɛma Ɛsau be la.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Too, baŋ du de la, Dɛbɔra la n dɛɛzi Ribɛka la siu. Denii ba kpau hug daazeŋ bɛl zɛɛ n du Bɛtɛl wiifurii nodig wiikakag. Awia, ba faa dau a daa sɔŋ Alɔn Bakuf.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: N daŋ kpa hɛri la miŋ faa kpawɛɛ tɛɛ Abraham ni Aizik la di tɛi. N daŋ kpau di tɛɛ i naanna gba.”

ref: Jacob set up a large rock, so that he would remember what had happened there. Then he poured wine and olive oil on the rock to show that it was dedicated to God,
text: Jekɔb faa kpa bii baŋ, ú ra liizi bɔr la Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔ wia tɛu la. Di yiili siŋ ni nuu di waari u ni,

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: di ra yir de Bɛtɛl. Rachɛl ni Aizik siu wia

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Denii ba faa kaa izi Bɛtɛl di ra la Ɛfraf. Ka baŋ ga hel galga, Rachɛl lulaa hiwiila lɔu, u ra nyiŋŋi u lul, a nuuu werge.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Unna di a dɔgnya gunla, u hahpaari ŋɔ u be, “Gilɛɛ ra ɔm, biibaallɛ i kaa ra lul.”

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Denii u ra siu, ka dau u bie hiŋ sɔŋ Bɛn Oni, ka u heeza jaa tuŋ. A bie mɛɛ ge faa dau u sɔŋ Bɛnjamin.

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Ɛfraf ŋmɛnɛɛrɛ Rachɛl faa siu, ba huguu de. Denii ba jaa ra yir Bɛtlihɛm filaŋkeŋ la.

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Jekɔb faa kpa bii di baŋ u ŋmam hiŋ. U haa baŋ de haali ni zinaa.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Jekɔb faa kaa lii de di vol guŋŋ di ga so Ɛda diijaŋ la har.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Saŋa la un du de la, Rubin lau u hahjiga Bilha di chɔɔ. U nuu guŋ. Jekɔb faa dɔɔ biibaala fidibanɛɛrɛ.

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Jekɔb la u mɛɛ Aizik be u bol Mamrɛ. Denii dugli Kiriaf Aaba la banna yir bolzeŋ Hɛbrɔn la. Abraham gba faa sogo denii.

ref: Esau, also known as Edom, had many descendants.
text: Ɛsau naanna wiarɛ keŋ. U sɔŋ bɛllɛ faa Ɛdɔm.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Ɛsau faa kpa haana banɛɛrɛ di lii Keenan hɛri. Bɛllɛ Hitti hinuu Ɛlɔn biihaaŋ Ada. Bɛl genii Ana biihaaŋ Ɔhɔlibama la n faa ɛɛ Hivi hinuu Zibiɔn naana.

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: Di faa kaa kpa u mɛɛ Ismɛl biihaaŋ Basɛmaf la n faa ɛɛ Nɛbayɔf niuhambie la.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Ɛsau ni Ɔhɔlibama ge lul Jɛyus, Jalam ani Kɔra. Keenan hɛrii Ɛsau faa lul biibaala naa maa.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Denii Ɛsau faa izi di kpogli u haana, u biizi ani u diahina maa, di kpa u kundɛɛzaa ani u kundɔɔlaa la maa un nawɛɛ lii Keenan hɛri la, di kaali hɛri bɛl ka ka u niwee Jekɔb,

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: dikuubɛɛwiai, de hɛri la faa waa kaŋ bá ni ba sekpila la maa du de.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Awia, Ɛsau faa la ga so giŋgimee bɔr n du Sɛɛ hɛri, ba ra yir de Ɛdɔm.

ref: Esau and Adah's oldest son was Eliphaz, and the clans that descended from him were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text: Banniibuu faa ɛɛ koora, Ɛsau naanna biini. Ɛsau biibambilugli Ɛlifaz biibaalaa, Tɛman, Omar, Zɛfɔ, Kɛnaz,

ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: Kɔra, Gatam ani Amalɛk. Koora naabuu faa lii Ɛlifaz naanna biini, Ɛdɔm hɛri. Ba faa ɛɛ Ada naannaa.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Basemath's son Reuel were Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
text: Ɛsau biibaal Riwɛl la ge biibaalaa faa, Nahaf, Zɛra, Samma ani Miza ba genii faa koora, Ɛdɔm hɛri. Ba ge faa ɛɛ Ɛsau haaŋ Basɛmaf naanna.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Ɛsau ni u haaŋ Ɔhɔlibama ge biibaalaa faa, Koora Jɛyus, Jalam ani Kɔra. Baabuu faa Ɛsau haaŋ Ɔhɔlibama la n ɛɛ Ana biihaaŋ la naanna.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Bannii naa maa ɛɛ Ɛsau la banna yir Ɛdɔm la deere.

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lɔtan biibaalaa faa, Hɔri ani Hɔmam. U niuhambie ge sɔnnɛ faa Timna.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Sobal ge biibaalaa faa, Alvan, Manahaf, Ɛbal, Sɛfɔ ani Ɔnam.

ref: Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah—the same Anah who found an oasis in the desert while taking the donkeys of his father out to pasture.
text: Zibiɔn ge biibaalaa faa, Aaya ni Ana. Ana naabuu faa na bulwema hɛribɔduma biini, di u faa ra nɔɔni u mɛɛ Zibiɔn kauzi.

ref: Anah's children were Dishon and Oholibamah.
text: Ana biizii faa, Disɔn ani u niuhambie Ɔhɔlibama.

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Disɔn ge biibaalaa faa, Hɛmdan, Ɛsban, Itran ani Kɛran.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Ɛza biibaalaa faa, Bilhan, Zaavan ani Akan.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Disan ge biibaalaa faa, Uz ani Aran

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Hɔri hina kooraa faa, Lɔtan, Sobal, Zibiɔn, Ana,

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Disɔn, Ɛza ani Disan, di lii ba dizi biini, Ɛdɔm hɛri. Ɛdɔm koora wia

ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Akbɔ biibaal Baal Hanan n wa siuwɛ, Hadad kpa u gawa, di di a kora. Ba faa dau u bolzeŋ sɔŋ Pau. U haaŋ sɔnnɛ faa Mɛhɛtabɛl, u ɛɛ Matrɛd biihaaŋ. Matrɛd ge maarɛ faa Mɛ Zahab.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau took their names from their families and the places where they lived. They are Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text: Koora naabuu faa lii Ɛsau naanna biini. Ba sɔnaa keŋ di hiŋzi ba domeezi ni ba hɛssi. Timna, Alva, Jɛtɛf,

ref: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text: Ɔhɔlibama, Ɛla, Pinɔn,

ref: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text: Kɛnaz, Tɛman, Mibzar,

ref: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived,
text: Jekɔb faa haa so Keenan hɛri, bɔr la u mɛɛ n faa soge mɔɔ la.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Ba waa kaa hoo ɛŋ. N nuuwɔ baŋ ŋɔwɛɛ lugɔ ba ra la Dɔtannɛ.” Denii Joosɛf faa dau ba luge, di ga na baa Dɔtan.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Jekɔb faa nɔŋŋuubuu di kaali u kpɛɛnna la maa, dikuu uŋ faa zɛŋŋɛ ka haa luluu la wia. Denii u kpa tagtajannanii bɛl di tɛu.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: U kpɛɛnna n nawɛɛ lugɔ ba mɛɛ nɔŋŋuubuu di kaali baa la, ba jaa hauh werge maa. Ba waa kaa ra ŋɔ wiŋɔnanaa di ra tɛu.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Denii Joosɛf deezi, di baglaa tɛɛ u kpɛɛnna. Guŋ faa gil ba hauh werge maa di jaa.

ref: Joseph said, “Let me tell you about my dream.
text: U faa ŋɔ ba benii, “Ií ra nuu.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: N deeza hiŋ biini, n na yaa n du koo di ra vɔɔzi mia. Anla miŋ vɔɔzɛɛ la izi di chi chiŋ, ii hor vɔɔzaa la chaali di wa baruu, di ra tognuu.”

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Denii u kpɛɛnna la ŋɔ, “I ra fɛu lugɔ hinuu niŋ ŋmɛnɛ wa ɛɛ yaa kooriaa?” Ba faa waa kaa ra nyiŋŋi u wia tama maa, dikuu u deeza hiŋ ani gunla uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la wia.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Denii a hɔl bɛl u kaa deezi, di ŋɔ u kpɛɛnna la be, “Ií kaa nuu n deeza naa. Wiizi ni baagu ani chinchiŋwilaa fidikpaŋ tognoore di ra jaamiŋ!”

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”
text: U faa bagli u deeza hiŋ waarɛ di tɛɛ u mɛɛ gba. U mɛɛ hau u ni ka pɛɛzuu, “Beedee gunnɛi? I ra bagluu lugɔ n ni i maa ani i kpɛɛnna daŋ ŋmɛnɛ wa tognuu di ra jaamiii?”

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Joosɛf wia faa ra wii u kpɛɛnna la ni werge maa, ka u mɛɛ faa kpaa du u biini, ka ra binni gunla n niŋ wa ɛɛ. Joosɛf kpɛɛnna n yoou wia

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: A hɔl bɛl, Joosɛf kpɛɛnna la Sɛkɛm bá ga nɔɔni ba mɛɛ peeza.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Denii Jekɔb ŋɔ Joosɛf be, “I zummɔ lugɔ i kpɛɛnna du Sɛkɛm di ra nɔɔni peeza. N daŋ humii ba be.” Joosɛf ŋɔ, “N daŋ la.”

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: U mɛɛ ŋɔ, “La di ga binni ba ni a peeza di na gunla baŋ duwɛɛ, ka bir ba di wa ŋɔ n be.” Denii u mɛɛ humuu di lii Hɛbrɔn Waluu biini. Joosɛf n ga hel Sɛkɛm,

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: baal bɛl nau di u vollo ra juuri, u pɛɛzuu, “Beedee i ra nyiŋŋei?”

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Joosɛf ŋɔ, “N kpɛɛnnaa n na nyiŋŋi. I zum bɔr la baŋ duwɛɛ?”

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Joosɛf kpɛɛnna la n wa nau di u haa du bɔɔnɛɛ di ra ba, ba ta jina lugɔ bá kpuu.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Ba ŋɔ dɔŋa be, “Deeza hiŋ labuu ra ba la.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Rubin n wa nuu guŋ, u ra nyiŋŋi u lɛɛu, di ŋɔ, “Ií gilɛɛ gil ya kpuu.

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Ií gil ya lawuu ha kɔlɔŋ naa biini, ka gilɛɛ waa kpuu.” Rubin faa ŋɔ gunnɛ ú vii wer lɛɛu ba be, di kpogluu ga tɛɛ u mɛɛ.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Joosɛf n wa hel ba be, ba lawuu manaŋ, di lau u tagtajannanii la u mɛɛ n tɛu la di wir,

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Ban tau du de la, ba jaa so di ra di. Ba wa ŋɔ ba binni di nyugmazii gaa ba. Ismɛl kɔkɔɔzaa faa la Giliad di ga yau dɔzi tiuzaa kuna, nuuri aŋhina ani mɛr, di ra la Ijip.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Denii Juda ŋɔ u dɔŋahina la be, “Di yaa kpu yaa niwee hiŋ di suah, beedee tɔnaa ya nai?

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Awia, ií gil ya lizuu di yoo tɛɛ Ismɛl kɔkɔɔza naa. Ií gilɛɛ gil ya lɛɛ u haahkɛ. U ɛɛ ya niweere.” Denii u lulla la maa sɛɛ lugɔ ba ɛɛ guŋ.

ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
text: Midian hina kɔkɔɔza la n wa hel ba be, ba lizuu di yoo saliŋhuŋŋa kebisau tokko di tɛɛ baa, ba jaa kpogluu kaali Ijip.

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Rubin n bittɛ la a kɔlɔŋ ka wɔɔ ga nawɛɛ la, a guŋ faa ɛɛu biinichɔg werge maa, u lau u tagta yeŋ piili.

ref: Then he went back to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”
text: Denii u faa bir la u niuna la be, di ga ŋɔ, “A bie waa kaa hoo de. Ɛrɛɛ ń jaa ɛɛ naanɛi?”

ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Denii Joosɛf kpɛɛnna na manaŋ di lau buuŋ kpu, di kpa u chal fɔg Joosɛf tagtajannanii la,

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”
text: ka jaa kpau di la ba mɛɛ be, di ga ŋɔ, “Pir biinii yaa na tagtajaŋ naa. Pessuu na, di Joosɛf tagtaarɛ gɛɛ waa lɛiyɛi?”

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Ba mɛɛ n wa nau la, u zumuu manaŋ, di ŋɔ, “Wibii maa, n biibaal tagtajannanii labuu. Sechɔgii bɛllɛ lau Joosɛf di kpuu la. U chuuri Joosɛf maanii!”

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Denii Jekɔb lau u laala piili, ka kpa gbɔglɔŋ tagta laali, di faa wii Joosɛf lee hɔpizikpila maa.

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Haali u biizi maa ɔɔ ɛɛ ania ba tig u bampirii, ka waa wette u suuh u wia, di ɔɔ ŋɔ, “N daŋ wii n biibaal naa lee guŋŋ di ra ba n siu.” Awia, u haa vɛɛ di ra wii u biibaal hiŋ lee.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Annii naa maa n na ɛɛ la, di Midian hina kɔkɔɔza la ga yoo Joosɛf tɛɛ Ijip koori huŋhunna nabɔma bɛl banna yir Pɔtifa. Pɔtifa faa ɛɛ Ijip kooridia binna nabɔmaa.

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: A guŋ saŋa hiŋ, Juda lii u lulla hiŋ be di la ga so baal bɛl banna yir Hira be, bol bɛl banna yir Adulam.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Denii u huuzi Keenan hina biihaaŋ n faa ɛɛ Sua biihaaŋ, di kpau.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: Sua kpogli hia di lul biibaal, Juda dau u sɔŋ Ɛr.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: U kaa lul horohiŋ, u dau u ge sɔŋ Sɛla. A Sɛla lulaa saŋa, di ba jaa du bol la banna yir Kɛzib.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Denii u nyiŋŋi haaŋ bɛl banna yir Taama, di tɛɛ u biinabɔma Ɛr, u kpa.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Ka Ɛr faa ɛɛ biinikpola hiŋ werge maa Kɔraawiizi be. U kuu guŋ wia di kpuu.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Denii Juda faa ŋɔ Ɛr niwee Ɔnan be, “Gil i ni i kpɛɛma haaŋ Taama chɔɔ, í ɛɛ gunla u jaari n dɛkkɛ u ɛɛ tɛu.”

ref: Onan knew the child would not be his, and when he had sex with Tamar, he made sure that she would not get pregnant.
text: Ka Ɔnan faa zum lugɔ a biizi wàá ɛɛ u hora. Awia, di u ni Taama faa haŋ wa chɔɔ maa, waa ɔɔ sɛɛ u banni zuu u ni, dikuubɛɛwiai, waa nyiŋŋi Taama wa kpogli hia di lul biizi, bá ɛɛ u kpɛɛma hiŋ hora.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: Kɔraawiizi faa na lugɔ Ɔnan ɛɛ biinikpola hinnɛ werge maa. U kaa kpu u ge.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Ɔnan n wa siuwɛ, Juda ŋɔ u naahaaŋ Taama be, “Filaŋkeŋ, kpogli i luhanni di ga du i mɛɛ dia guŋŋ n biibaal Sɛla wa hel haaŋ kpai.” U faa ŋɔ gunnɛ, dikuubɛɛwiai, u faa ra ɔmuu lugɔ Sɛla ge gba daŋ siu alugɔ u kpɛɛnna la. Denii Taama bir la u mɛɛ dia di ga du de.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Aŋ wa birgee tama, Juda haaŋ Sua la siu. Uŋ wa wii u lee tuŋ, Juda ni u dammu Adulam hinuu Hira la Timna, bɔr la banna fuŋ u peeza huna la.

ref: Tamar found out that her father-in-law Judah was going to Timnah to shear his sheep.
text: Denii nɛbɛl ga ŋɔ tɛɛ Taama lugɔ u jaari mɛɛ ra la Timnaa, ú ga fuŋ u peeza huna.

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: Taama na lugɔ Sɛla helloo tuŋ, ka Juda waa u wia ra ŋɔ lugɔ ú kpau. Denii u wir u luhanni laala, ka kpa saata bɛl tɔ lugɔ bá gilɛɛ zumuu, ka jaa lii di ga so Ɛnam n du Timna ŋmɛnɛɛ dominua.

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Denii Juda wa nau, ka fɛ lugɔ sensɛnni bɛllɛ, dikuu uŋ kpa saata di tɔ u siwee la wia.

ref: and asked her to sleep with him. She asked, “What will you give me if I do?”
text: U la u be a ŋmɛnikɛrii di ga ŋɔ ú nuu chɔɔ. Haali a guŋ saŋa maa di u haa waa zummɛ lugɔ u biibaal haannɛ. Taama pɛɛzuu, “Aŋmɛnɛɛ í turginnei?”

ref: “One of my young goats,” he answered. “What will you give me to keep until you send the goat?” she asked.
text: Juda ŋɔ, “N daŋ lau buuŋ di wa turgii.” U kaa ŋɔ, “Beedee kunnɛ i kpa tɛŋ mɔɔ ń zum lugɔ i daŋ wa tɛŋ a buunnɛi?”

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Juda bir ŋɔ, “Too, beedee waarɛ i ra nyiŋŋei?” Taama kaa ŋɔ, “N na nyiŋŋi kuŋ la hiŋ ɔɔ kpa di ra jag hɔna, ni u ŋmɛŋ ani i daŋkpala naa hiŋ kpoglee naa.” Denii Juda kpa kuna la maa uŋ baglɛɛ la di tɛu. U jaa sɛɛ u nuu chɔɔ. Ban chɔɔwɛ la u kpogli hia.

ref: After returning home, Tamar took off the veil and dressed in her widow's clothes again.
text: Uŋ wa kaalɛɛ la, u ga lau u saata la wir, ka kpa u luhanni laala la di laali.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Juda ge bir la ka ga gil u dammu Adulam hinuu la lau a buuŋ di la ú ga tɛu, ka lɛɛ u kuna la uŋ ŋɔ nua di kpa tɛɛ a haaŋ la wa tɛu. Ka wɔɔ ga nawɛɛ.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Denii u pɛɛzi baala baŋhina n du de, “Lee sɛnsɛnni la n faa du a bol naa ŋmɛnikɛrii la duɛi?” Ba bir ŋɔ u be, “Yaa waa sɛnsɛnni maa dɔɔwɛ bɔnnaa.”

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Hira jaa bir la ga ŋɔ Juda be lugɔ wɔɔ nawɛɛ. Haali de hina gba ŋɔ lugɔ ba waa sɛnsɛnni maa dɔɔwɛ de.

ref: “If you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,” Judah answered. “And we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.”
text: Denii Juda ŋɔ, “Gil u kpogli a kuna. Yaa ɛɛ aniaa di lau a buuŋ ya vii ga tɛu, ka yaa wɔɔ nawɛɛ. Di yaa bittɔ la de, ba daŋ gaa mam yaa.”

ref: About three months later someone told Judah, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has behaved like a prostitute, and now she's pregnant!” “Drag her out of town and burn her to death!” Judah shouted.
text: Aŋ wa ɛɛ baaguzi ahoro, ba tili Juda be lugɔ u biibaal haaŋ Taama sɛmmɔɔ di kpogli hia. Denii Juda ŋɔ, “Aa! Ií lawuu ba di wa fuuuh.”

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Ka baŋ wa lawuu di ra la ba ga kpu la, u tili di ŋɔ Juda be, “Baal la maa kuŋ n ɛɛ hɔna jagrikuŋ ni u ŋmɛŋ ani daŋkpala naabuu duŋ a hia. I zum a hiŋaa?”

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Juda sɛɛ lugɔ u kunaa, ka ŋɔ, “U hor summɔɔ, dikuubɛɛwiai, maa n nua la miŋ ŋɔwɛɛ lugɔ u daŋ yuu Sɛla la hiŋŋɛ.” Ama, waa kaa sɛɛ u nuu chɔɔ maa.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Unna lul la, bɛl nonii feli lii, haaŋ la n lawuu la kpa gessiaahmu di u nonii hiŋ, ka ŋɔ, “Unniibuu felee lii.”

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Ka uŋ wa bittɛ di lau u nonii du, unla hiŋ bir feli di wa lii.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Denii a haaŋ ŋɔ, “I fittɔɔ lii.” Ba faa dau u sɔŋ Pɛrɛz. Ba kaa dau unla ge gessi n vɔɔ u nonii la sɔŋ, Zɛra.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ismɛl kɔkɔɔza la kpogli Joosɛf kaali Ijip. Pɔtifa la n ɛɛ Ijip koori kooridia binna nabɔma yawuu.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Denii u dɔɔri la n wa zummɛ lugɔ Kɔraawiizi du Joosɛf benii, u wia maa dɔɔ siwee la,

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: u wia paruu werge maa, u jaa kpau birgi u himmaa huŋhunni. U kpa Joosɛf di birgi nɛr la n na binni u diahina wiimaa ni, di faa kpa u kundɔɔlaa maa du u nonee.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Denii Pɔtifa faa jaa kpa kunla maa un dɔɔwɛ fɔŋŋa di du Joosɛf nonee. Joosɛf n du u be la, wiimaa faa waa kaa tiruu, di diwee la unna di bɛŋ wia lɛi. Joosɛf mai faa wesso nɛnnɛ, u kaa ɛɛ bunɔɔŋ,

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Denii Pɔtifa haaŋ faa bir di ra nyiŋŋuu, di ŋɔ ú nuu chɔɔ.

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Ka Joosɛf vɛɛ, di ŋɔ u be, “N dɔɔri yardimbuu, di gil n na binni kummaa n du dia naa biini. Awiarɛ waa kaa ra du a tau la.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Nɛr maa waa a fɔŋŋa dɔɔwɛ di kaaliŋ. Kummaanii u kpa tɛŋ di i bɛŋ lɛi. U haannɛ hiŋ. Ɛrɛɛ ń wer ɛɛ keŋ widɔŋai? Guŋ ɛɛ wichɔgaarɛ werge maa, Kɔraawiizi be.”

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Haali uŋ ɔɔ ŋɔ Joosɛf be guŋ hɔpizi bii maa gba, ka Joosɛf waa ɔɔ sɛɛ, u nuu chɔɔ gɛɛ u du u be gba.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Denii a hɔl bɛl u zuu a dia ú hum u huma, di a dia balii bɛl maa waa hoo.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: A haaŋ la ga lau u tagtajaŋ di ŋɔ, “Gil n nii chɔɔ!” Joosɛf waa sɛɛwɛ, ka wir u tagtajaŋ hiŋ di du u nonee, ka chɔ lii bossee.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: u yir u diahina, di ŋɔ, “Ií na gunla Hibru hina yombaal naa n jaari n yauwɛ naa n ɔɔ hanna binni yaa pɛɛ, haali u wa zuu ŋɔ ú pumiŋ, ka n lɔ wile werge maa.

ref: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.”
text: Miŋ jaa lɔ a wile hiŋ, u wir u tagtajaŋ du n nonee, ka chɔ lii bossee.”

ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: U kpa a tagtajaŋ hiŋ di dau. U jaari n wa ballɛ,

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: u kpa guŋ bagli tɛu, di ŋɔ, “Hibru hina yombaal naa hiŋ lauwɛ ba naa binnimbuu pɛɛ, haali di wa zuu n be,

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: n lɔ wile u wir u tagtajaŋ du n nonee ka chɔ lii.”

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: U lau Joosɛf di tɔ dia, bɔr la u dɔɔri Ijip koori la n na tɔ nɛra la. Joosɛf n du a nɛra tɔi dia hiŋ,

ref: the Lord helped him and was good to him. He even made the jailer like Joseph so much that
text: Kɔraawiizi faa haa du u benii di ra hiinuu u wiɛɛlaa maa biini, u wia ra par nɛr la n na binni banla baŋ tɔ dia la ni.

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Aŋ wa ɛɛ tama, u gil Joosɛf ɛɛ u dɔŋahina la baŋ tɔ dia la maa nabɔma, di ra du wii la maa n na ɛɛ a dia biini tau.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: A nɛra tɔi dia binni la faa waa kaa ra fɔŋ wia la maa uŋ kpawɛɛ du Joosɛf nonee ŋɔ ú ra binni wia, Kɔraawiizi faa du u benii, di gil u wiimaa ra vol nɛnnɛ.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: u lau baa di tɔ dia, u dia binna nabɔma Pɔtifa dia, bɔr la baŋ tɔ Joosɛf la.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Ban du a nɛra tɔi dia la, Pɔtifa gil Joosɛf ra du ba tau. Ban du de hiŋ,

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: a hɔl bɛl hamuŋa ni, koori libikpogli ni u paanuɔɔzi chɔɔ deezi, bɛl maa deeza zɛɛ kur.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ, Joosɛf ba ba be, u na lugɔ ba biine chɔkkɔɔ.

ref: and he asked, “Why are you so worried today?”
text: U pɛɛzi baa, “Beedee n kpogli ia zinaa, ii nyɛm gunnɛi?”

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Denii ba ŋɔ, “Yaa dɛɛ deezoore, ka nɛr maa waa hoo ú bagli a zɛɛzi tɛɛ yaa.” Joosɛf ŋɔ, “Deeza zɛɛzi baglaa lii Kɔraawiizi benii. Ií bagliŋ ii deeza hiŋ.”

ref: The king's personal servant told Joseph, “In my dream I saw a vine
text: A koori libikpogli la ŋɔ, “N na grepu daa bɛllɛ n chigɛ n see,

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: di dɔɔ kela ahoro. U ni waraa di u fiiwɔ, u ni u fii di u nɔnnɔ, u nɔna aah.

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: N kaa na lugɔ miŋ kpogli Ijip koori libee. Denii n kpa nɔna la di hanzi a niiri di du a libee biini, di kpa tɛɛ Ijip koori.”

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Joosɛf ŋɔ, “N zum i deeza hiŋ zɛɛrɛ. Kela ahoro la ɛɛ hɔpizaa ahoroo.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Hɔpizaa ahoro biini, a koori daŋ lizii lii nɛra tɔi dia naa biini, di gil i kaa ra kpogli u libee, ú ra nyɔɔ gunla hiŋ faa ɔɔ ra kpogluu di ra tɛu saŋa maa la.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Awia, di i wa liwɔɔ, i wiimaa kuari nɛnnɛ, ɛɛ ra liizi n wia di ɛɛŋ nɛnnɛ, di gil Ijip koori zum n wia di liziŋ bɔnnaa.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: N kipar lɛi ba lii n ni Hibru hɛri di ba ɛŋ. Maa wii maa ɛɛwɛ di dɛg lugɔ bá tɔŋ dia gba.”

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Too, a paanuɔɔza nabɔma la n wa nawɛɛ lugɔ lugli hiŋ la deeza zɛɛ wessoo la, u ge bagli u deeza tɛɛ Joosɛf, di ŋɔ, “N deezoore di na alugɔ miŋ chigɛ paanuuzi kɛɛgɔɔzi ahoro.

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: Unla n hɔŋ chiŋ la su paanuɔɔzaa irizi miŋ chigɛ di ra la ń ga tɛɛ Ijip koori. Ka zumbizi ba di waa dia.”

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Joosɛf kaa ŋɔ, “N daŋ bagli a ge zɛɛ tɛi. Kɛɛgɔzi ahoro la ɛɛ hɔpizaa ahoroo.
